subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests of two objects designed to solve the same problem to compare the strengths and weaknesses of how each performs.
description_plain: Your child can look at test results from two objects made to solve the same problem and compare what each does well or not.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple sketch, drawing, or physical model to illustrate how the shape of an object helps it function as needed to solve a given problem.
description_plain: Your child can draw or build a simple model to show how an object's shape helps it work to solve a problem.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions, make observations, and gather information about a situation people want to change to define a simple problem that can be solved through the development of a new or improved object or tool.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions and gather information to understand a problem people want to fix by making a new or better tool.

subject: Science
description: Communicate solutions that will reduce the impact of humans on the land, water, air, and/or other living things in the local environment.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas to help reduce how people harm the land, water, air, or living things nearby.

subject: Science
description: Use a model to represent the relationship between the needs of different plants or animals (including humans) and the places they live.
description_plain: Your child will use a model to show how different plants and animals (including people) need certain things and places to live.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument supported by evidence for how plants and animals (including humans) can change the environment to meet their needs.
description_plain: Your child can explain with examples how plants and animals (including people) change their surroundings to meet their needs.

subject: Science
description: Use observations to describe patterns of what plants and animals (including humans) need to survive.
description_plain: Your child will watch and describe what plants and animals (including people) need to live and grow.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to obtain information about the purpose of weather forecasting to prepare for, and respond to, severe weather.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to learn why weather forecasts help people get ready for bad weather.

subject: Science
description: Use and share observations of local weather conditions to describe patterns over time.
description_plain: Your child will watch and share what the weather is like in your area to notice patterns over time.

subject: Science
description: Use tools and materials to design and build a structure that will reduce the warming effect of sunlight on an area.
description_plain: Your child can use tools and materials to build something that helps keep an area cooler by blocking sunlight.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to determine the effect of sunlight on Earth's surface.
description_plain: Your child will observe and describe how sunlight affects the Earth's surface.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data to determine if a design solution works as intended to change the speed or direction of an object with a push or a pull.
description_plain: Your child can look at data to decide if a design works to change how fast or which way an object moves when pushed or pulled.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to compare the effects of different strengths or different directions of pushes and pulls on the motion of an object.
description_plain: Your child will plan and do an experiment to see how different pushes and pulls affect how an object moves.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out fair tests in which variables are controlled and failure points are considered to identify aspects of a model or prototype that can be improved.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do fair tests to find parts of a model or prototype that can be made better.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple possible solutions to a problem based on how well each is likely to meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and compare several possible solutions to a problem to see which one fits best.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem reflecting a need or a want that includes specified criteria for success and constraints on materials, time, or cost.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple design problem that includes what success looks like and limits like materials, time, or cost.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe the movement of matter among plants, animals, decomposers, and the environment.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to show how matter moves between plants, animals, decomposers, and the environment.

subject: Science
description: Support an argument that plants get the materials they need for growth chiefly from air and water.
description_plain: Your child can explain that plants get most of what they need to grow from air and water.

subject: Science
description: Use models to describe that energy in animals' food (used for body repair, growth, motion, and to maintain body warmth) was once energy from the sun.
description_plain: Your child can use models to show that the energy animals get from food originally comes from the sun.

subject: Science
description: Conduct an investigation to determine whether the mixing of two or more substances results in new substances.
description_plain: Your child can do an experiment to find out if mixing substances creates new substances.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and measurements to identify materials based on their properties.
description_plain: Your child can observe and measure materials to identify them by their properties.

subject: Science
description: Measure and graph quantities to provide evidence that regardless of the type of change that occurs when heating, cooling, or mixing substances, the total weight of matter is conserved.
description_plain: Your child can measure and graph amounts to show that matter's weight stays the same during heating, cooling, or mixing.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe that matter is made of particles too small to be seen.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to show that matter is made of tiny particles too small to see.

subject: Science
description: Represent data in graphical displays to reveal patterns of daily changes in length and direction of shadows, day and night, and the seasonal appearance of some stars in the night sky.
description_plain: Your child can create graphs to show daily changes in shadows, day and night, and how some stars appear differently during seasons.

subject: Science
description: Support an argument that differences in the apparent brightness of the sun compared to other stars is due to their relative distances from Earth.
description_plain: Your child can explain why the sun looks brighter than other stars because it is closer to Earth.

subject: Science
description: Support an argument that the gravitational force exerted by Earth on objects is directed down.
description_plain: Your child can explain that Earth's gravity pulls objects straight down.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information about ways individual communities use science ideas to protect the Earth's resources and environment.
description_plain: Your child can find and combine information about how communities use science to protect Earth's resources and environment.

subject: Science
description: Describe and graph the amounts and percentages of water and fresh water in various reservoirs to provide evidence about the distribution of water on Earth.
description_plain: Your child can describe and graph amounts and percentages of water in different places to show how water is spread on Earth.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model using an example to describe ways the geosphere, biosphere, hydrosphere, and/or atmosphere interact.
description_plain: Your child can build a model to show how land, living things, water, and air work together.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests of two objects designed to solve the same problem to compare the strengths and weaknesses of how each performs.
description_plain: Your child can compare test results from two objects made to solve the same problem to see what each does well or not.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple sketch, drawing, or physical model to illustrate how the shape of an object helps it function as needed to solve a given problem.
description_plain: Your child can make a simple drawing or model to show how an object's shape helps it work to solve a problem.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions, make observations, and gather information about a situation people want to change to define a simple problem that can be solved through the development of a new or improved object or tool.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions and gather information to understand a problem people want to fix by making a new or better tool.

subject: Science
description: Make observations at different times of year to relate the amount of daylight to the time of year.
description_plain: Your child can watch how the amount of daylight changes during different times of the year.

subject: Science
description: Use observations of the sun, moon, and stars to describe patterns that can be predicted.
description_plain: Your child can observe the sun, moon, and stars to find patterns that happen again and again.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account that young plants and animals are like, but not exactly like, their parents.
description_plain: Your child can observe young plants and animals and explain how they are similar but not exactly like their parents.

subject: Science
description: Read texts and use media to determine patterns in behavior of parents and offspring that help offspring survive.
description_plain: Your child can read and watch videos to learn how parents and their babies behave in ways that help the babies survive.

subject: Science
description: Use materials to design a solution to a human problem by mimicking how plants and/or animals use their external parts to help them survive, grow, and meet their needs.
description_plain: Your child can use materials to create a solution to a problem by copying how plants or animals use their body parts to survive and grow.

subject: Science
description: Use tools and materials to design and build a device that uses light or sound to solve the problem of communicating over a distance.
description_plain: Your child can design and build a tool that uses light or sound to help people communicate from far away.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to determine the effect of placing objects made with different materials in the path of a beam of light.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an experiment to see what happens when light shines on objects made of different materials.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account that objects can be seen only when illuminated.
description_plain: Your child can observe and explain that we can only see objects when light shines on them.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct investigations to provide evidence that vibrating materials can make sound and that sound can make materials vibrate.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do experiments to show that vibrating things make sound and sound can make things vibrate.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to generate data for iterative testing and modification of a proposed object, tool, or process such that an optimal design can be achieved.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to test and improve a design step by step to find the best solution.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests to determine similarities and differences among several design solutions to identify the best characteristics of each that can be combined into a new solution to better meet the criteria for success.
description_plain: Your child can study test data to find the best parts of different designs and combine them into a better solution.

subject: Science
description: Evaluate competing design solutions using a systematic process to determine how well they meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can carefully compare different design ideas to see how well they meet the problem’s needs and limits.

subject: Science
description: Define the criteria and constraints of a design problem with sufficient precision to ensure a successful solution, taking into account relevant scientific principles and potential impacts on people and the natural environment that may limit possible solutions.
description_plain: Your child can clearly describe what a design problem needs and what limits there are, thinking about science and effects on people and nature.

subject: Science
description: Use mathematical representations to support explanations of how natural selection may lead to increases and decreases of specific traits in populations over time.
description_plain: Your child can use math to explain how natural selection can cause certain traits in a group of animals or plants to increase or decrease over time.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation based on evidence that describes how genetic variations of traits in a population increase some individuals' probability of surviving and reproducing in a specific environment.
description_plain: Your child can explain how differences in traits help some individuals survive and have babies better in their environment.

subject: Science
description: Analyze displays of pictorial data to compare patterns of similarities in the embryological development across multiple species to identify relationships not evident in the fully formed anatomy.
description_plain: Your child can look at pictures showing early development in different animals to find similarities that show how they are related.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific ideas to construct an explanation for the anatomical similarities and differences among modern organisms and between modern and fossil organisms to infer evolutionary relationships.
description_plain: Your child can explain how similarities and differences in body parts of living and fossil organisms show their family relationships.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data for patterns in the fossil record that document the existence, diversity, extinction, and change of life forms throughout the history of life on Earth under the assumption that natural laws operate today as in the past.
description_plain: Your child can study fossil data to understand how life on Earth has changed, including new species, extinction, and diversity.

subject: Science
description: Conduct an investigation and evaluate the experimental design to provide evidence that fields exist between objects exerting forces on each other even though the objects are not in contact.
description_plain: Your child can do experiments to show that forces can act between objects even when they are not touching.

subject: Science
description: Construct and present arguments using evidence to support the claim that gravitational interactions are attractive and depend on the masses of interacting objects.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to explain that gravity pulls objects together and depends on their masses and distance apart.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions about data to determine the factors that affect the strength of electric and magnetic forces.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions about data to find out what affects how strong electric and magnetic forces are.

subject: Science
description: Plan an investigation to provide evidence that the change in an object's motion depends on the sum of the forces on the object and the mass of the object.
description_plain: Your child can plan an experiment to show that how an object’s motion changes depends on the total forces on it and its mass.

subject: Science
description: Apply Newton's Third Law to design a solution to a problem involving the motion of two colliding objects.
description_plain: Your child can use Newton’s Third Law to design a solution for a problem involving two objects hitting each other.

subject: Science
description: Integrate qualitative scientific and technical information to support the claim that digitized signals (sent as wave pulses) are a more reliable way to encode and transmit information.
description_plain: Your child can explain why digital signals (sent as wave pulses) are a more reliable way to send information.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe that waves are reflected, absorbed, or transmitted through various materials.
description_plain: Your child can build and use a model to show how waves can bounce off, be absorbed by, or pass through different materials.

subject: Science
description: Use mathematical representations to describe a simple model for waves that includes how the amplitude of a wave is related to the energy in a wave.
description_plain: Your child can use math to describe a simple wave model, including how wave height relates to the wave’s energy.

subject: Science
description: Construct a scientific explanation based on evidence from rock strata for how the geologic time scale is used to organize Earth's 4.6-billion-year-old history.
description_plain: Your child can explain how scientists use layers of rock to organize Earth’s 4.6-billion-year history.

subject: Science
description: Gather and synthesize information about the technologies that have changed the way humans influence the inheritance of desired traits in organisms.
description_plain: Your child can gather information about technologies that help humans change traits in living things.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe why structural changes to genes (mutations) located on chromosomes may affect proteins and may result in harmful, beneficial, or neutral effects to the structure and function of the organism.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show how changes in genes (mutations) can affect proteins and cause good, bad, or no changes in an organism.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe that when the arrangement of objects interacting at a distance changes, different amounts of potential energy are stored in the system.
description_plain: Your child can make a model to show how changing the positions of objects that interact from a distance changes the stored energy.

subject: Science
description: Construct and interpret graphical displays of data to describe the relationships of kinetic energy to the mass of an object and to the speed of an object.
description_plain: Your child can create and read graphs to explain how an object’s kinetic energy depends on its mass and speed.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to determine scale properties of objects in the solar system.
description_plain: Your child can study data to understand the sizes and distances of objects in our solar system.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe the role of gravity in the motions within galaxies and the solar system.
description_plain: Your child can build and use a model to explain how gravity controls the movement of galaxies and the solar system.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model of the Earth-sun-moon system to describe the cyclic patterns of lunar phases, eclipses of the sun and moon, and seasons.
description_plain: Your child can create and use a model of the Earth, sun, and moon to explain patterns like moon phases, eclipses, tides, and seasons.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out fair tests in which variables are controlled and failure points are considered to identify aspects of a model or prototype that can be improved.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do tests where they keep things fair and think about what might go wrong to find ways to make a model or prototype better.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple possible solutions to a problem based on how well each is likely to meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and compare different solutions to a problem by thinking about how well each one meets the needs and limits of the problem.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem reflecting a need or a want that includes specified criteria for success and constraints on materials, time, or cost.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple design problem by explaining what is needed or wanted and what rules there are about materials, time, or cost.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation for how the variations in characteristics among individuals of the same species may provide advantages in surviving, finding mates, and reproducing.
description_plain: Your child understands how differences in animals or plants help them survive, find mates, and have babies.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to support the explanation that traits can be influenced by the environment.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to show how the environment can change traits in living things.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to provide evidence that plants and animals have traits inherited from parents and that variation of these traits exists in a group of similar organisms.
description_plain: Your child can look at data to show that plants and animals get traits from their parents and that these traits can vary.

subject: Science
description: Develop models to describe that organisms have unique and diverse life cycles but all have in common birth, growth, reproduction, and death.
description_plain: Your child can create models to show that all living things have life stages like birth, growth, reproduction, and death.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem that can be solved by applying scientific ideas about magnets.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple problem that can be solved using ideas about magnets.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to determine cause and effect relationships of electric or magnetic interactions between two objects not in contact with each other.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to find out how electric or magnetic forces affect objects without touching.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and/or measurements of an object's motion to provide evidence that a pattern can be used to predict future motion.
description_plain: Your child can watch and measure how things move to find patterns that help predict future motion.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to provide evidence of the effects of balanced and unbalanced forces on the motion of an object.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an experiment to show how balanced and unbalanced forces change how things move.

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a solution to a problem caused when the environment changes and the types of plants and animals that live there may change.
description_plain: Your child can explain if a solution is good for helping plants and animals when their environment changes.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument with evidence that in a particular habitat some organisms can survive well, some survive less well, and some cannot survive at all.
description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence why some animals live well in a habitat, some live less well, and some cannot live there at all.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from fossils to provide evidence of the organisms and the environments in which they lived long ago.
description_plain: Your child can study fossils to learn about animals and environments from long ago.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that some animals form groups that help members survive.
description_plain: Your child can explain why some animals live in groups to help each other survive.

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a design solution that reduces the impacts of a weather-related hazard.
description_plain: Your child can explain why a design helps reduce problems caused by bad weather.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe climates in different regions of the world.
description_plain: Your child will gather and combine information to describe different climates around the world.

subject: Science
description: Represent data in tables and graphical displays to describe typical weather conditions expected during a particular season.
description_plain: Your child can show weather data in tables and graphs to explain what weather is usually like in a season.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to generate data for iterative testing and modification of a proposed object, tool, or process such that an optimal design can be achieved.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to collect data by testing and changing a design many times to find the best version.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests to determine similarities and differences among several design solutions to identify the best characteristics of each that can be combined into a new solution to better meet the criteria for success.
description_plain: Your child can study test results to find what is similar and different in several designs and pick the best parts to make a better new design.

subject: Science
description: Evaluate competing design solutions using a systematic process to determine how well they meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can carefully compare different design ideas to see how well each meets the problem’s needs and limits.

subject: Science
description: Define the criteria and constraints of a design problem with sufficient precision to ensure a successful solution, taking into account relevant scientific principles and potential impacts on people and the natural environment that may limit possible solutions.
description_plain: Your child can clearly explain the needs and limits of a design problem, thinking about science and how it might affect people and the environment.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data on the distribution of fossils and rocks, continental shapes, and seafloor structures to provide evidence of the past plate motions.
description_plain: Your child can look at data about fossils, rocks, continents, and the ocean floor to show how Earth's plates have moved in the past.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation based on evidence for how geoscience processes have changed Earth's surface at varying time and spatial scales.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, how Earth's surface has changed over different times and places because of natural processes.

subject: Science
description: Evaluate competing design solutions for maintaining biodiversity and ecosystem services.
description_plain: Your child can compare different design ideas for protecting biodiversity and ecosystem services and decide which work best.

subject: Science
description: Construct an explanation that predicts patterns of interactions among organisms across multiple ecosystems.
description_plain: Your child can explain and predict how living things interact with each other in different ecosystems.

subject: Science
description: Undertake a design project to construct, test, and modify a device that either releases or absorbs thermal energy by chemical processes.
description_plain: Your child can design, build, test, and improve a device that uses chemical reactions to give off or take in heat.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe how the total number of atoms does not change in a chemical reaction and thus mass is conserved.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show that the total number of atoms stays the same during a chemical reaction, so mass doesn’t change.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data on the properties of substances before and after the substances interact to determine if a chemical reaction has occurred.
description_plain: Your child can look at data about substances before and after they react to decide if a chemical reaction happened.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data on natural hazards to forecast future catastrophic events and inform the development of technologies to mitigate their effects.
description_plain: Your child can study data about natural disasters to predict future events and help create ways to reduce their damage.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument supported by empirical evidence that changes to physical or biological components of an ecosystem affect populations.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to explain how changes in an ecosystem’s physical or living parts affect animal and plant populations.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe the cycling of matter and flow of energy among living and nonliving parts of an ecosystem.
description_plain: Your child can build a model to show how matter cycles and energy flows between living things and their environment.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to provide evidence for the effects of resource availability on organisms and populations of organisms in an ecosystem.
description_plain: Your child can study data to show how the amount of resources affects living things and their populations in an ecosystem.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe how food is rearranged through chemical reactions forming new molecules that support growth and/or release energy as this matter moves through an organism.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to explain how food changes through chemical reactions to help an organism grow or get energy.

subject: Science
description: Construct a scientific explanation based on evidence for the role of photosynthesis in the cycling of matter and flow of energy into and out of organisms.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, how photosynthesis helps matter cycle and energy flow in and out of living things.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model that predicts and describes changes in particle motion, temperature, and state of a pure substance when thermal energy is added or removed.
description_plain: Your child can make a model that predicts how particles move, temperature changes, and states of matter change when heat is added or taken away.

subject: Science
description: Gather and make sense of information to describe that synthetic materials come from natural resources and impact society.
description_plain: Your child can gather and understand information to explain that man-made materials come from natural resources and affect society.

subject: Science
description: Develop models to describe the atomic composition of simple molecules and extended structures.
description_plain: Your child can build models to show what atoms make up simple molecules and larger structures.

subject: Science
description: Construct a scientific explanation based on evidence for how the uneven distributions of Earth's mineral, energy, and groundwater resources are the result of past and current geoscience processes.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, how Earth's uneven spread of minerals, energy, and water comes from past and current Earth processes.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe the cycling of Earth's materials and the flow of energy that drives this process.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to show how Earth's materials cycle and how energy moves to drive this process.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests of two objects designed to solve the same problem to compare the strengths and weaknesses of how each performs.
description_plain: Your child can look at test results from two objects made to solve the same problem and compare what each does well or not so well.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple sketch, drawing, or physical model to illustrate how the shape of an object helps it function as needed to solve a given problem.
description_plain: Your child can draw or build a simple model to show how an object's shape helps it work to solve a problem.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions, make observations, and gather information about a situation people want to change to define a simple problem that can be solved through the development of a new or improved object or tool.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, watch carefully, and gather information to describe a simple problem that can be solved by making a new or better tool.

subject: Science
description: Obtain information to identify where water is found on Earth and that it can be solid or liquid.
description_plain: Your child can find out where water is on Earth and understand that water can be ice or liquid.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to represent the shapes and kinds of land and bodies of water in an area.
description_plain: Your child can create a model that shows the shapes and types of land and water in a certain place.

subject: Science
description: Compare multiple solutions designed to slow or prevent wind or water from changing the shape of the land.
description_plain: Your child can compare different ways people try to stop wind or water from changing the land’s shape.

subject: Science
description: Use information from several sources to provide evidence that Earth events can occur quickly or slowly.
description_plain: Your child can use information from different places to show that Earth changes can happen fast or slowly.

subject: Science
description: Make observations of plants and animals to compare the diversity of life in different habitats.
description_plain: Your child can watch plants and animals and compare how many different kinds live in different places.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple model that mimics the function of an animal in dispersing seeds or pollinating plants.
description_plain: Your child can make a simple model that shows how an animal helps spread seeds or pollinate plants.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to determine if plants need sunlight and water to grow.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an experiment to see if plants need sunlight and water to grow.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument with evidence that some changes caused by heating or cooling can be reversed and some cannot.
description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence which changes caused by heating or cooling can be undone and which cannot.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account of how an object made of a small set of pieces can be disassembled and made into a new object.
description_plain: Your child can watch and describe how an object made of a few parts can be taken apart and made into something new.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data obtained from testing different materials to determine which materials have the properties that are best suited for an intended purpose.
description_plain: Your child can test different materials and decide which ones work best for a certain use.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to describe and classify different kinds of materials by their observable properties.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an experiment to describe and sort materials by what they look and feel like.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out fair tests in which variables are controlled and failure points are considered to identify aspects of a model or prototype that can be improved.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do fair tests where they control variables and think about what might fail to find ways to improve a model or prototype.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple possible solutions to a problem based on how well each is likely to meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and compare different solutions to a problem by thinking about how well each meets the needs and limits.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem reflecting a need or a want that includes specified criteria for success and constraints on materials, time, or cost.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple design problem by explaining what is needed or wanted and the limits on materials, time, or cost.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe that energy and fuels are derived from natural resources and their uses affect the environment.
description_plain: Your child will gather information to explain that energy and fuels come from natural resources and that using them can affect the environment.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific ideas to design, test, and refine a device that converts energy from one form to another.
description_plain: Your child can design, test, and improve a device that changes energy from one type to another.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions and predict outcomes about the changes in energy that occur when objects collide.
description_plain: Your child will ask questions and guess what will happen to energy when objects bump into each other.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to provide evidence that energy can be transferred from place to place by sound, light, heat, and electric currents.
description_plain: Your child can watch and describe how energy moves from one place to another through sound, light, heat, and electricity.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation relating the speed of an object to the energy of that object.
description_plain: Your child will use evidence to explain how the speed of something relates to its energy.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple solutions that use patterns to transfer information.
description_plain: Your child thinks of and compares different ways patterns can be used to send information.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model of waves to describe patterns in terms of amplitude and wavelength and that waves can cause objects to move.
description_plain: Your child can build a simple model to show how waves move, including their height and length, and explain how waves can make things move.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple solutions to reduce the impacts of natural Earth processes on humans.
description_plain: Your child comes up with and compares different ideas to lessen how natural Earth events impact people.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from maps to describe patterns of Earth's features.
description_plain: Your child looks at maps and data to describe patterns in Earth’s features.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and/or measurements to provide evidence of the effects of weathering or the rate of erosion by water, ice, wind, or vegetation.
description_plain: Your child observes or measures how weathering or erosion by water, ice, wind, or plants changes the land.

subject: Science
description: Identify evidence from patterns in rock formations and fossils in rock layers to support an explanation for changes in a landscape over time.
description_plain: Your child uses patterns in rocks and fossils to explain how a landscape has changed over time.

subject: Science
description: Use a model to describe that animals' receive different types of information through their senses, process the information in their brain, and respond to the information in different ways.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show how animals get information through their senses, process it in their brain, and respond in different ways.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that plants and animals have internal and external structures that function to support survival, growth, behavior, and reproduction.
description_plain: Your child explains how plants and animals have body parts inside and outside that help them survive, grow, behave, and reproduce.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe that light reflecting from objects and entering the eye allows objects to be seen.
description_plain: Your child makes a model to show that we see objects because light reflects off them and enters our eyes.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to generate data for iterative testing and modification of a proposed object, tool, or process such that an optimal design can be achieved.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to collect data by testing and changing a design many times to find the best version.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests to determine similarities and differences among several design solutions to identify the best characteristics of each that can be combined into a new solution to better meet the criteria for success.
description_plain: Your child can study test results to find what is similar and different in several designs and pick the best parts to make a better new design.

subject: Science
description: Evaluate competing design solutions using a systematic process to determine how well they meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can carefully compare different design ideas to see how well each meets the problem’s needs and limits.

subject: Science
description: Define the criteria and constraints of a design problem with sufficient precision to ensure a successful solution, taking into account relevant scientific principles and potential impacts on people and the natural environment that may limit possible solutions.
description_plain: Your child can clearly explain the needs and limits of a design problem, thinking about science and how it might affect people and the environment.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to clarify evidence of the factors that have caused the rise in global temperatures over the past century.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to better understand the reasons why global temperatures have risen over the last 100 years.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe how unequal heating and rotation of the Earth cause patterns of atmospheric and oceanic circulation that determine regional climates.
description_plain: Your child can build and use a model to show how uneven heating and Earth's spinning cause patterns in air and ocean movement that affect climate.

subject: Science
description: Collect data to provide evidence for how the motions and complex interactions of air masses results in changes in weather conditions.
description_plain: Your child can collect data to show how moving air masses and their interactions cause changes in weather.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument supported by evidence for how increases in human population and per-capita consumption of natural resources impact Earth's systems.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to explain how more people and higher use of natural resources affect Earth's systems.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific principles to design a method for monitoring and minimizing a human impact on the environment.
description_plain: Your child can design a way to watch and reduce how people affect the environment.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe why asexual reproduction results in offspring with identical genetic information and sexual reproduction results in offspring with genetic variation.
description_plain: Your child can create and use a model to explain why babies from asexual reproduction are identical and babies from sexual reproduction are different.

subject: Science
description: Construct a scientific explanation based on evidence for how environmental and genetic factors influence the growth of organisms.
description_plain: Your child understands how both the environment and genes affect how living things grow, using evidence to explain.

subject: Science
description: Use argument based on empirical evidence and scientific reasoning to support an explanation for how characteristic animal behaviors and specialized plant structures affect the probability of successful reproduction of animals and plants respectively.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, how animal behaviors and special plant parts help animals and plants reproduce successfully.

subject: Science
description: Gather and synthesize information that sensory receptors respond to stimuli by sending messages to the brain for immediate behavior or storage as memories.
description_plain: Your child can gather and combine information to show how senses send messages to the brain to cause quick actions or memories.

subject: Science
description: Use argument supported by evidence for how the body is a system of interacting subsystems composed of groups of cells.
description_plain: Your child can explain, with evidence, how the body works as a system made of smaller parts called cells.

subject: Science
description: Develop and use a model to describe the function of a cell as a whole and ways parts of cells contribute to the function.
description_plain: Your child can build and use a model to show how a cell works as a whole and how its parts help it function.

subject: Science
description: Conduct an investigation to provide evidence that living things are made of cells, either one cell or many different numbers and types of cells.
description_plain: Your child can do an experiment to prove that all living things are made of one or many cells.

subject: Science
description: Construct, use, and present arguments to support the claim that when the kinetic energy of an object changes, energy is transferred to or from the object.
description_plain: Your child can explain and support the idea that when an object's movement energy changes, energy moves to or from that object.

subject: Science
description: Plan an investigation to determine the relationships among the energy transferred, the type of matter, the mass, and the change in the average kinetic energy of the particles as measured by the temperature of the sample.
description_plain: Your child can plan an experiment to find out how energy transfer, type of matter, mass, and temperature changes are related.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific principles to design, construct, and test a device that either minimizes or maximizes thermal energy transfer.
description_plain: Your child can design, build, and test a device that either reduces or increases heat transfer.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe the cycling of water through Earth's systems driven by energy from the sun and the force of gravity.
description_plain: Your child can create a model to show how water moves through Earth's systems using energy from the sun and gravity.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and audiences, allowing time to think and improve.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and audiences, allowing time to think and improve.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from informational texts to support thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from informational texts to support thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple authoritative print and digital sources, using advanced searches effectively; assess the usefulness of each source in answering the research question; integrate information into the text selectively to maintain the flow of ideas, avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can gather useful information from trusted print and online sources, decide which is best for their question, and use it properly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple authoritative print and digital sources, using advanced searches effectively; assess the usefulness of each source in answering the research question; integrate information into the text selectively to maintain the flow of ideas, avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can gather useful information from trusted print and online sources, decide which is best for their question, and use it properly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question) or solve a problem; narrow or broaden the inquiry when appropriate; synthesize multiple sources on the subject, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects to answer questions or solve problems, adjusting the focus as needed and combining information from many sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question) or solve a problem; narrow or broaden the inquiry when appropriate; synthesize multiple sources on the subject, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects to answer questions or solve problems, adjusting the focus as needed and combining information from many sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce, publish, and update individual or shared writing products, taking advantage of technology's capacity to link to other information and to display information flexibly and dynamically.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to create, share, and update writing, including linking to other information and showing ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce, publish, and update individual or shared writing products, taking advantage of technology's capacity to link to other information and to display information flexibly and dynamically.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to create, share, and update writing, including linking to other information and showing ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on addressing what is most significant for a specific purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to focus on what matters most for their audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on addressing what is most significant for a specific purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to focus on what matters most for their audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and logically with style and organization that fit the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and logically with style and organization that fit the task, purpose, and audience.

description: Not applicable as a separate requirement.
description_plain: Your child will not have a separate requirement for this standard.

description: Not applicable as a separate requirement.

description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented (e.g., articulating implications or the significance of the topic).
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows from the information or explanation they gave.

description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows from the information or explanation they gave.

description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone while attending to the norms and conventions of the discipline in which they are writing.
description_plain: Your child can keep a formal and objective tone in their writing, following the rules of the subject they are writing about.

description_plain: Your child can use clear and subject-specific words to explain complex topics in a way that fits the subject and readers.

description_plain: Your child describes the main characters and settings in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate comprehension of age and grade-appropriate texts by speaking or writing.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand grade-level texts by talking or writing about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use background knowledge and details, including illustrations, charts, and graphs, to make predictions and inferences about what happens in a text.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know and details like pictures to guess what might happen next in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build general and academic vocabulary and background knowledge of age and grade-appropriate topics through discussion, reading, and writing.
description_plain: Your child learns new words and background information by talking, reading, and writing about age-appropriate topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell (orally or in writing) a text read aloud, including main idea and key details to demonstrate understanding of the central message or topic.
description_plain: Your child retells a story or text by sharing the main idea and important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child answers questions about important details in a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions about important details when reading a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally read texts with accuracy, automaticity, and expression at an appropriate rate to support comprehension, self-correcting as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can read aloud smoothly and with feeling, fixing mistakes to understand the text better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce manuscript writing with accuracy and automaticity.
description_plain: Your child can write letters neatly and quickly by hand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form upper and lowercase manuscript letters and numerals correctly with proportion, line awareness, and spacing (between words and letters).
description_plain: Your child can write uppercase and lowercase letters and numbers correctly with good spacing and size.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode words with inflectional suffixes that do not require a spelling change (e.g., jumps, jumped, jumping).
description_plain: Your child can spell words with endings like -s, -ed, and -ing without changing the base word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.
description_plain: Your child can spell words by sounding them out using letter sounds they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode one-syllable words with the most common spellings for final long vowel sounds (e.g., say, play, cry, why, tree, snow).
description_plain: Your child can spell one-syllable words with common long vowel sounds like 'say' and 'tree'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode one-syllable words with predictable vowel-r patterns (e.g., shark, fork, her).
description_plain: Your child can spell one-syllable words with vowel + r sounds like 'shark' and 'fork'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode one-syllable words with initial or medial VCe patterns (e.g., use, lake, slime, choke, these).
description_plain: Your child can spell one-syllable words with vowel-consonant-e patterns like 'lake' and 'choke'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode final /v/ with -ve (e.g., give, love, have, five, solve, swerve).
description_plain: Your child can spell words that end with a /v/ sound using -ve, like 'give' and 'love'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode one-syllable words containing single consonants with multiple spellings for /z/ and /k/ (e.g., is, nose, zip; kite, cat, luck).
description_plain: Your child can spell one-syllable words with different spellings for /z/ and /k/ sounds, like 'zip' and 'cat'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode one-syllable words with double-letter endings (e.g., fill, pass, buzz).
description_plain: Your child can spell one-syllable words that end with double letters, like 'fill' and 'buzz'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode one-syllable words with consonant digraphs and short vowel patterns (e.g., with, flash, chick, shaft, song).
description_plain: Your child can spell one-syllable words with two-letter sounds like 'sh' and 'ch', such as 'flash' and 'chick'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode one-syllable words with consonant clusters and short vowel patterns (e.g., flat, drift, quit, stand, frost, sprint, strap).
description_plain: Your child can spell one-syllable words with groups of consonants and short vowels, like 'flat' and 'drift'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with inflectional suffixes (e.g., -ed, -ing, -s/es, -er).
description_plain: Your child can read words with endings like -ed, -ing, -s, and -er.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read high-frequency words.
description_plain: Read high-frequency words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode two-syllable base words with grade level letter-sound correspondences (e.g., napkin, cupcake, poodle, super).
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable words using letter sounds they know, like 'napkin' and 'cupcake'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode one-syllable words containing multiple sounds of s (e.g., sock, is).
description_plain: Your child can read one-syllable words with different 's' sounds, like 'sock' and 'is'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode one-syllable words with diphthongs (e.g., oi, oy, ou, ow).
description_plain: Your child can read one-syllable words with vowel sounds like 'oi', 'oy', 'ou', and 'ow'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode one-syllable words with common vowel teams (e.g., rain, play; seed, beach; high, pie; boat, snow, toe; book; new, moon).
description_plain: Your child can read one-syllable words with common vowel pairs like 'rain', 'seed', and 'boat'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode one-syllable words with consonant digraphs (e.g., sh, th, ch, wh, ng, ck) and trigraphs (e.g., tch, dge) with predictable vowel patterns (e.g., shy, chart, shave, thick, white, bring, catch, fudge).
description_plain: Your child can read one-syllable words with special two- or three-letter sounds like 'sh', 'th', and 'tch', such as 'shy' and 'catch'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode one-syllable words with consonant clusters and predictable vowel patterns (e.g., blast, spell, dry, flake, star, swerve).
description_plain: Your child can read one-syllable words with groups of consonants and common vowel sounds, like 'blast' and 'star'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between sounds based on place of articulation (where in the mouth it is produced) and manner of articulation (voicing and air flow) (e.g., /p/ vs. /b/; /ch/ vs. /j/).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between sounds based on where and how they are made in the mouth, like /p/ vs. /b/.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment the sounds in one-syllable words (e.g., sip to /s/ /i/ /p/; slip to /s/ /l/ /i/ /p/; sift to /s/ /i/ /f/ /t/; blast to /b/ /l/ /a/ /s/ /t/).
description_plain: Your child can break apart the sounds in one-syllable words, like saying /s/ /i/ /p/ for 'sip'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend three to five sounds in one-syllable words (e.g., /s/ /i/ /p/ to sip; /s/ /l/ /i/ /p/ to slip; /s/ /i/ /f/ /t/ to sift; /b/ /l/ /a/ /s/ /t/ to blast).
description_plain: Your child can put together 3 to 5 sounds to say one-syllable words, like /s/ /i/ /p/ to say 'sip'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas before a coordinating conjunction in a compound sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use commas before words like 'and' or 'but' when joining two sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in closing of letters.
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly at the end of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings.
description_plain: Use commas in greetings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas with words and items in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas to separate words or items in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use all end punctuation marks.
description_plain: Your child can use all kinds of punctuation marks at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize product names.
description_plain: Capitalize product names.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays.
description_plain: Capitalize holidays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand and combine simple sentences to create compound sentences, using two independent clauses.
description_plain: Your child can join two simple sentences to make one sentence with two parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conjunctions (and, but, or) in compound sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'and', 'but', and 'or' to join sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adverbs.
description_plain: Use adverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use subject/verb agreement for singular and plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child can make sure verbs match singular or plural subjects correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use irregular verbs (e.g., sang, came).
description_plain: Your child can use irregular verbs like 'sang' and 'came' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize words adjectives modify.
description_plain: Your child can recognize which words adjectives describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common adjectives.
description_plain: Use common adjectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use pronoun/antecedent agreement.
description_plain: Your child can use pronouns that match the nouns they replace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use possessive pronouns (e.g., mine, his, hers)
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'mine', 'his', and 'hers' to show ownership.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use subject pronouns (e.g., I, he, she).
description_plain: Your child can use subject pronouns like 'I', 'he', and 'she' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular possessive common and proper nouns.
description_plain: Your child can use singular possessive nouns to show something belongs to one person or thing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use regular and irregular plural nouns (e.g., branches, elves; geese, sheep).
description_plain: Your child can use regular and irregular plural nouns like 'branches' and 'geese'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns (e.g., love, peace, joy).
description_plain: Your child can use words that name feelings or ideas, like 'love' and 'peace'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak audibly in collaborative communication and presentations.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly and loudly enough when talking with others or giving presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use visual displays to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, charts, or drawings to explain their ideas, feelings, or thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relevant, descriptive details to orally share a story or experience that includes appropriate facts.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or share an experience out loud using clear and interesting details and facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says to gather additional information and clarify understanding.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone else says to learn more and understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations following class created discussion guidelines to expand upon conversations.
description_plain: Your child can join group talks by following agreed rules and adding to the conversation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research projects to gather information from experiences and/or provided sources to produce writing.
description_plain: Your child can work with others to find information from experiences or sources and use it to write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Edit writing to include K-2 language conventions for publishing.
description_plain: Your child can fix their writing by checking spelling, punctuation, and grammar before sharing it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing to expand and combine simple sentences to create compound sentences.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by making simple sentences longer and joining them together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use transitional words to connect ideas.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'and,' 'but,' or 'because' to connect ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize writing logically, constructing an introduction, body, and a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can organize their writing with a beginning, middle, and ending that makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing, using precise language, relevant details and descriptions, and grade-appropriate conventions.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly using exact words, important details, and correct grammar for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a narrative, using a logical sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can write a story that follows events in the right order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write an informative or explanatory piece about a topic, using facts from a source.
description_plain: Your child can write about a topic by sharing facts they learned from a source.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write an opinion piece about a topic or text with details to support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child can write their opinion about a topic or book and give reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of base words (including compound words) and common prefixes and suffixes (e.g., un-, re-, pre-, dis-, -ly, -able) as clues to meaning (e.g., add, addition, additional).
description_plain: Your child can use parts of words like base words, prefixes, and suffixes to figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the real meaning and the special or hidden meaning of words in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs (e.g., toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (e.g., thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).
description_plain: Your child can notice small differences in similar action words and describing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort and categorize words by multiple attributes (e.g., synonyms, antonyms, size, color, type).
description_plain: Your child can group words by their meanings, opposites, size, color, or type.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level clues to identify the meaning of words or phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use clues in a sentence to understand the meaning of new words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text read aloud or independently.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or explain what words and phrases mean when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the author's purpose of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell why an author wrote a text, like to inform, entertain, or persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific visual images (e.g., charts, graphs, illustrations) contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures, charts, or graphs help make the text clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features (e.g., headings, table of contents, glossaries, bold font, italics) to locate key facts and information.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of a book like headings, table of contents, or bold words to find important facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the purpose of a section of a text (e.g., paragraphs, sections).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the purpose of different parts of a text, like paragraphs or sections.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare two texts about the same topic and tell how they are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons/evidence to support main ideas in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and facts to support the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of a story and the purpose of the parts (e.g., paragraphs, sections, chapters, stanzas, lines).
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story is organized and what the parts like chapters or stanzas do.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify differences in the perspectives of characters.
description_plain: Your child can tell how different characters think or feel differently in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific visual images contribute to and clarify story elements (setting, characters, and events).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures in a story help show the setting, characters, and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two versions of the same story (e.g., Three Little Pigs vs. The True Story of the Three Little Pigs).
description_plain: Your child can compare two versions of the same story and explain how they are different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main story elements (characters, settings, events, problem, and solution) in a text.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main parts of a story like who is in it, where it happens, what happens, the problem, and the solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe a character's traits.
description_plain: Your child can describe what a character is like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate reading comprehension of age and grade-appropriate texts by speaking or writing.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand what they read by talking or writing about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use background knowledge and details, including illustrations, charts, and graphs, to make predictions and inferences about what happens in a text.
description_plain: Your child can use what they already know and details like pictures or charts to guess what will happen next in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build general and academic vocabulary and background knowledge of age and grade-appropriate topics through discussion, reading, and writing.
description_plain: Your child can learn new words and ideas about topics by talking, reading, and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell (orally or in writing) a text read aloud including main idea and key details to demonstrate understanding of the central message or topic.
description_plain: Your child can retell a story or text by telling the main idea and important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can answer questions about important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions about important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally read texts with accuracy, automaticity, and expression at an appropriate rate to support comprehension, self-correcting as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can read out loud with accuracy, expression, and at a good speed, fixing mistakes when needed to understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form upper and lowercase cursive letters correctly, using a common path of movement.
description_plain: Your child can write uppercase and lowercase cursive letters correctly using smooth movements.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode one and two-syllable words with common prefixes and suffixes, applying spelling changes [e.g., -e drop (game to gaming); doubling (run to running); change y to i (cry to cries)] to the base word when necessary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode two-syllable words with consonant-le (Cle) (e.g., candle, noble, steeple, startle, giggle, sparkle).
description_plain: Your child can spell two-syllable words that end with consonant-le, like candle and giggle.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode one-syllable words with diphthongs (i.e., oi, oy, ou, ow).
description_plain: Your child can spell one-syllable words that have diphthong sounds like oi, oy, ou, and ow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode one-syllable words with common vowel teams (e.g., rain, pay; beach; high, pie; boat, snow, toe; book; new, moon).

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode long vowel patterns (e.g., wild, told, kind, most, colt).

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode final /s/ spelled as ss, ce, and se (e.g., dress, face, moose).

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode two-syllable words with short vowels (e.g., candid, combat).
description_plain: Your child can spell two-syllable words with short vowel sounds, like candid and combat.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode one-syllable words with consonant trigraphs and short vowel patterns (e.g., catch, judge).
description_plain: Your child can spell one-syllable words with consonant trigraphs and short vowels, like catch and judge.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode with common prefixes and suffixes (e.g., un-, re-, -ly, -able).

description: Decode two-syllable base words with grade level letter-sound correspondences (e.g., dollar, wrestle, kidney).
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable base words using grade-level letter sounds, like dollar and kidney.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode one-syllable words with vowel teams (e.g., haul, straw, clue, chief, key, bread).

description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words.

description: Generate synonyms and antonyms for common nouns, verbs, and adjectives.
