subject: Language Arts
description: Use context clues to identify the meaning of words or phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from sentences to figure out what unknown words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text read aloud or independently.
description_plain: Your child can find or explain the meaning of words and phrases when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the reader's perspective from that of the author of the text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own thoughts and the author's ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how information gained from illustrations (e.g., maps, photographs) and the words in a text, contribute to understanding a text.
description_plain: Your child explains how pictures, maps, or photos help understand the words in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and keywords, sidebars, and hyperlinks to locate information relevant to a given topic.
description_plain: Your child uses things like headings, side notes, and links to find information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how each successive part or paragraph builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child describes how each part or paragraph adds to what was said before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two texts on the same topic, identifying key details from each text.
description_plain: Your child compares two texts on the same topic and finds important details in each.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support specific points in a text.
description_plain: Your child explains how an author uses reasons and facts to support ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the major differences in the structural elements of poems, dramas, and stories.
description_plain: Your child can describe how poems, plays, and stories are different in their parts and structure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the reader's perspective from that of the author, narrator, or character(s).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own views and those of the author, narrator, or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how information gained from visual images in a text contributes to understanding the text.
description_plain: Your child explains how pictures in a story help understand what is happening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast stories from the same author with the same or similar characters.
description_plain: Your child compares stories by the same author that have the same or similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the main story elements (plot), including characters, setting, events, problem, and solution.
description_plain: Your child can describe the main parts of a story, like characters, setting, events, problem, and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters respond to major events and challenges.
description_plain: Your child describes how characters react to big events and problems in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate reading comprehension of age and grade-appropriate texts by speaking or writing.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand what they read by talking or writing about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use background knowledge and details, including illustrations, charts, and graphs, to make inferences about what happens in a text.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know and pictures or charts to guess what happens next in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build general and academic vocabulary and background knowledge of age and grade-appropriate topics through discussion, reading, and writing.
description_plain: Your child learns new words and background information by talking, reading, and writing about grade-level topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize multi-paragraph texts, providing key details to demonstrate understanding of the central message or topic.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main idea and important details from longer texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child answers questions about important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child asks questions about important details when reading a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally read texts with accuracy, automaticity, and expression at an appropriate rate to support comprehension, self-correcting as necessary.
description_plain: Your child reads out loud smoothly, with expression and correct words, fixing mistakes when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce cursive writing with accuracy and automaticity.
description_plain: Your child writes in cursive accurately and automatically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write legibly in cursive, using correctly formed letters with appropriate slant, spacing, and line awareness.
description_plain: Your child writes neat cursive letters with the right slant, spacing, and on the lines.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode words, using knowledge of most common Latin prefixes, bases, and suffixes including inflectional and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child spells words using common Latin prefixes, bases, and suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode two-syllable words with vowel-r patterns (e.g., forget, doctor, cartoon, mustard).
description_plain: Your child spells two-syllable words with vowel-r sounds like 'doctor' and 'mustard.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode two-syllable words with medial long vowel teams (e.g., complain, canteen).

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode two-syllable words with double letter endings (e.g., repel vs. confess).
description_plain: Your child spells two-syllable words that end with double letters, like 'repel' and 'confess.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words, using knowledge of most common Latin prefixes, bases, and suffixes including inflectional and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child reads words by using common Latin prefixes, bases, and suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multisyllable base words with grade-level letter-sound correspondences (e.g., neighbor, suitcase, fascinate, suggest).
description_plain: Your child reads longer base words with grade-level letter sounds, like 'neighbor' and 'fascinate.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode one-syllable words with vowel teams (e.g., vein, eight, fruit, youth, prey).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use quotation marks in quotations from a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use quotation marks in dialogue.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in quotations from a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dialogue.
description_plain: Use commas in dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end marks in dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize dialogue.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct capitalization.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complex sentences, using dependent clauses and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child can write sentences that include extra information using words like because or when.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use contractions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use prepositional phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use interjections (e.g., eek, yikes).

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Use comparative and superlative adverbs (e.g., farther, farthest).
description_plain: Your child will use words that compare actions, like 'farther' or 'farthest'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use subject/verb agreement.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Use comparative and superlative adjectives (e.g., funnier, funniest).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use possessive pronouns as adjectives (e.g., their house, her dog).
description_plain: Your child will use words like 'their' or 'her' to show who owns something.

description_plain: Your child will use words like who, which, or that to connect parts of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak audibly, using standard English as appropriate to the task and/or situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use visual displays and/or audio appropriately to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details when presenting.
description_plain: Your child will use pictures, charts, or sounds to help explain important facts when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relevant, descriptive details to orally report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support specific points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations following class created guidelines to expand upon conversations.
description_plain: Your child will join group talks and follow rules to add more to the conversation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Take notes, sort evidence into categories, and include a list of sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research by gathering and paraphrasing information from relevant experiences and/or from sources to produce a written response.
description_plain: Your child will do short research by finding and putting information into their own words to write about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Edit writing to include K-4 language conventions for publishing.
description_plain: Your child will fix their writing to follow grammar and spelling rules before sharing it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and revise writing to convey ideas precisely.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use transitional words, phrases, and clauses to connect ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize writing logically, constructing an introduction, body, and conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing, using precise language, relevant details, elaboration, and grade-appropriate conventions.

description: Use knowledge of Latin prefixes, bases, and suffixes as clues to meaning (e.g., construct, instruct, deconstruction; structure, infrastructure; nation, nature, nativity).

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of specific academic language, including words and phrases that signal precise actions or emotions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between a word and its antonyms and synonyms to deepen understanding.
description_plain: Your child will use synonyms and antonyms to better understand word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials to clarify pronunciation and/or precise meaning of words.
description_plain: Your child will use dictionaries or other tools to check how to say words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context clues to infer the meaning of words or phrases.
description_plain: Your child will use clues from the sentence to figure out the meaning of unknown words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in texts read aloud or independently.
description_plain: Your child will figure out what words and phrases mean when reading or listening to stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support specific points in a text.

description_plain: Your child builds their vocabulary and background knowledge by talking, reading, and writing about different topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize multi-paragraph texts, providing details to demonstrate understanding of the central message or topic.
description_plain: Your child can summarize longer texts by telling the main idea and important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer explicit and inferential questions, using details from a text.
description_plain: Your child can answer questions about a text using details from what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child asks questions about important details in a text to understand it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally read texts with accuracy, automaticity, and expression, at an appropriate rate to support comprehension, self-correcting as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can read aloud smoothly, with expression and at a good speed, fixing mistakes as they go.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write fluently and legibly in cursive, using correctly formed letters with appropriate slant, spacing, and line awareness with increasing stamina.
description_plain: Your child can write neatly and quickly in cursive, forming letters correctly with good spacing and slant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Encode words, using knowledge of Greek combining forms with the use of connectives as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly by using knowledge of Greek word parts and joining words when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words, using knowledge of Greek combining forms and connectives.
description_plain: Your child can sound out words by recognizing common Greek word parts and how they connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of reading foundational skills, spelling patterns, and generalizations such as syllable patterns, ending rules, and meaningful word parts (i.e., morphology) to spell correctly.
description_plain: Your child will spell words correctly by using rules about syllables, endings, and word parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Indicate dialogue, quotes, and titles, using quotation marks.
description_plain: Your child can use quotation marks correctly for dialogue, quotes, and titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas to set off series, phrases and clauses, and direct address.
description_plain: Your child uses commas to separate items in a list, parts of sentences, and when speaking directly to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Set off restrictive, nonrestrictive, and parenthetical elements, using commas, parentheses, dashes.
description_plain: Your child uses commas, parentheses, or dashes to add extra information or clarify meaning in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce compound-complex sentences, using dependent clauses, subordinating conjunctions, conjunctive adverbs, correlative conjunctions, and coordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child can write complex sentences by joining ideas with words like because, although, and but.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use modifiers effectively:
  • Proper adjectives
  • Predicate adjectives
description_plain: Your child uses describing words correctly, including adjectives that come from proper nouns and those that describe the subject.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use verbs effectively:
  • Perfect verb tenses
  • Shifts in mood
  • Subject/verb agreement
  • Linking/be verbs
description_plain: Your child uses verbs correctly, including perfect tenses, changes in mood, matching subjects and verbs, and linking verbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure pronouns have a clear antecedent and are appropriate in number and person.
description_plain: Your child makes sure pronouns clearly refer to the right noun and match in number and person.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use pronouns properly:
  • Intensive and reflexive pronouns
  • Relative pronouns
description_plain: Your child uses special pronouns like myself, yourself, and who, which, that correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use nouns effectively:
  • Direct objects
description_plain: Your child uses nouns correctly as direct objects in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recite a passage from a well-known poem, play, or speech.
description_plain: Your child can recite a passage from a famous poem, play, or speech from memory.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child uses correct standard English when it is needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation in consideration of the audience.
description_plain: Your child speaks clearly with good eye contact, volume, and pronunciation for the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia and visuals to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child includes pictures, videos, or other media to help explain information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use accurate descriptions, facts, and/or details to support central ideas and/or themes.
description_plain: Your child uses accurate details and facts to support main ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sequence ideas logically.
description_plain: Sequence ideas logically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and evidence.
description_plain: Present claims and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a speaker's argument and supporting claims, distinguishing between claims that are supported by evidence from those not supported by evidence.
description_plain: Your child can identify a speaker’s main argument and tell which claims have evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how non-textual information enhances the understanding of a topic, text, or issue.
description_plain: Your child explains how pictures, charts, or other non-written information help understand a topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase multiple perspectives.
description_plain: Your child can restate different points of view in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Contribute to the topic, text, or issue, responding to questions with relevant information.
description_plain: Your child shares relevant information and answers questions about a topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build upon ideas in collaborative discussions, asking questions relevant to the topic, text, or issue.
description_plain: Your child builds on others’ ideas in group talks by asking questions related to the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Follow rules for collegial discussions, setting specific goals and deadlines and defining individual roles. Collegial discussions should include:
  • One-on-one
  • In small groups
  • Teacher-led
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group discussions, helps set goals and roles, and works well in pairs, small groups, or with a teacher.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, referring to gathered evidence on the topic, text, or issue.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared with facts and evidence about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Express ideas in a collaborative setting, using effective discussion strategies.
description_plain: Your child shares ideas clearly and uses good discussion skills when talking with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate an understanding of figurative language in context, including extended metaphor and personification.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figurative language like metaphors and personification in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how word choice contributes to the meaning, style, and/or tone of a text.
description_plain: Your child notices how the choice of words affects the meaning, style, or feeling of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize two words with similar denotations, considering how their connotations and nuances impact the words' meanings.
description_plain: Your child compares two similar words and explains how their feelings or shades of meaning are different.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Determine the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing from a range of effective techniques:
  • Use common Greek or Latin affixes and roots (i.e., morphology)
  • Trace the origins of words (i.e., etymology)
  • Use context
  • Consult reference materials to clarify pronunciation and/or parts of speech
  • Use word relationships such as cause and effect, part to whole, and item into category to clarify the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new or tricky words by using word parts, word origins, context clues, dictionaries, and word relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode and encode words, using knowledge of Greek combining forms and Latin prefixes, bases, and suffixes and connectives as needed.
description_plain: Your child can read and spell words by knowing Greek and Latin prefixes, roots, suffixes, and how they connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use general academic and content-specific words and phrases accurately.
description_plain: Your child uses general and subject-specific words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide basic and consistent bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child provides basic and consistent information about where they found their sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote or paraphrase data and conclusions, crediting sources and/or authors.
description_plain: Your child quotes or restates information from sources and gives credit to the authors.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess credible sources.
description_plain: Assess credible sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct research to answer a question.
description_plain: Your child researches to find answers to questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain formal style when appropriate, editing writing to include grade-appropriate conventions for publishing.
description_plain: Your child writes formally when needed and edits their work to follow grade-level rules before sharing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include headings and graphics to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child adds headings and pictures to make information clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Increase independent writing with support and collaboration from peers and adults, employing the stages of the writing process (e.g., draft, revise, edit) with a focus on purpose.
description_plain: Your child writes more on their own with help from others, using steps like drafting, revising, and editing focused on their purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop writing stamina during single sessions and over extended periods of time.
description_plain: Your child practices writing for longer times during single sessions and over several days.

subject: Language Arts
description: Construct clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to the task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organized, using a style that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence, to signal shifts from one time or setting to another, and/or to clarify the relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show order, changes in time or place, and connections between ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of sentence types (i.e., simple, compound, complex, compound-complex).
description_plain: Your child uses a mix of sentence types, including simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize writing logically, constructing an introduction, body, conclusion, and/or reflection when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can organize their writing with a clear beginning, middle, and end, including an introduction, body, conclusion, or reflection when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose precise words, phrases, and relevant details to accurately convey experiences, events, and/or information.
description_plain: Your child will choose exact words and details to clearly describe experiences, events, or information.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write to express real or imagined experiences and/or events.
  • Use relevant descriptive details.
  • Organize well-structured event sequences.
  • Use narrative techniques (e.g., dialogue, sequencing, description, characterization).
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using details, putting events in order, and including things like dialogue and character descriptions.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write to inform about a topic.
  • Organize ideas, concepts, and information.
  • Use relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, and quotations.
description_plain: Your child will write to explain a topic by organizing ideas and using facts, definitions, details, and quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write an argument, using clear reasons and supporting evidence.
  • Introduce claims.
  • Support claims with credible sources.
description_plain: Your child can write an argument by stating their opinion clearly and supporting it with good reasons and trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the central idea in a non-fiction work from or about world literature (beginnings of civilization through 1450), explaining its historical and/or contemporary significance.
description_plain: Your child understands the main idea of a nonfiction text about early world history and can explain why it matters then and now.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two authors' presentations of an event.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two authors tell the same event differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the development of an argument and supporting claims in a text, distinguishing between claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can tell which parts of an argument in a text have good reasons and evidence and which parts do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine a topic or issue in two or more multimedia formats (e.g., presentations, charts, images, audio).
description_plain: Your child can explore a topic using different types of media like pictures, charts, videos, or presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how an author's purpose, point of view, and/or perspective is conveyed in a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's purpose and point of view in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how a particular sentence or paragraph in a text fits into the overall structure and contributes to the development of an idea, theme, or argument.
description_plain: Your child understands how a sentence or paragraph fits into the whole text and helps develop an idea or argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a central individual, event, or idea is introduced and developed in a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a key person, event, or idea is introduced and explained in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the theme in an original, adapted, or modernized drama, poem, folktale, or story from world literature (beginnings of civilization through 1450), explaining its historical and/or contemporary significance.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme in stories, poems, or plays from early world literature and explain why it is important historically or today.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine two texts of varying structures or genres that have similar themes and/or topics.
description_plain: Your child can look at two different texts with similar themes or topics and compare them.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Compare and contrast the experience of reading a text to listening to or watching an audio, video, or live version of the text. Compare and contrast what is:
  • Imagined when reading a text.
  • Observed when listening and/or watching.
description_plain: Your child can compare reading a story to listening or watching it, noticing what they imagine versus what they see or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the point of view and/or perspective of the narrator or speaker in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows the narrator's or speaker's point of view in a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how elements in the structure of a story, drama, or poem support the development of setting, plot, or theme.
description_plain: Your child understands how parts of a story, play, or poem help build the setting, plot, or theme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a plot develops over the course of a text, including how the characters respond and/or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.
description_plain: Your child can describe how the plot of a story develops and how characters change or react as the story moves toward the ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate reading comprehension of age and grade-appropriate texts by speaking or writing.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand what they read by talking or writing about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite text evidence to support an analysis of what a text states, using background knowledge to draw inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find evidence in a text to support their ideas and use what they know to understand more than what is directly said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how a central idea and/or theme of a text is conveyed through supporting details.
description_plain: Your child understands how the main idea or theme of a text is shown through supporting details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide an objective summary of a text.
description_plain: Your child can give a clear and unbiased summary of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of reading foundational skills, spelling patterns, and generalizations such as syllable patterns, ending rules, and meaningful word parts (i.e., morphology) to spell correctly.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly by using rules about syllables, endings, and word parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Indicate dialogue, quotes, and titles, using quotation marks.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use quotation marks for dialogue, quotes, and titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Join elements of a series when individual items of the series already include commas, using a semicolon.
description_plain: Your child can use semicolons to separate items in a list when the items already have commas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas to separate coordinate adjectives, set off series, phrases and clauses, and direct address.
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly to separate adjectives, items in a series, phrases, and when speaking directly to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Set off restrictive, nonrestrictive, and parenthetical elements, using commas, parentheses, dashes.
description_plain: Your child can use commas, parentheses, and dashes to set off extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose language that precisely expresses ideas, eliminating redundancy.
description_plain: Your child can choose words that clearly express ideas and avoid repeating the same thing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify types of phrases and clauses based on their functions in sentences.
description_plain: Your child can identify different types of phrases and clauses by what they do in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce compound-complex sentences, using dependent clauses, subordinating conjunctions, conjunctive adverbs, correlative conjunctions, and coordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child can write complex sentences that combine ideas using different connecting words and clauses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correct misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child can fix sentences where modifiers are in the wrong place or unclear.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use modifiers effectively:
  • Proper adjectives
  • Predicate adjectives
description_plain: Your child can use adjectives correctly, including those that describe nouns and those that come after linking verbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbals (gerunds, participles, infinitives) correctly.
description_plain: Your child can use verb forms like gerunds, participles, and infinitives correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use verbs effectively:
  • Perfect verb tenses
  • Shifts in mood
  • Active and passive voice
  • Subject/verb agreement
  • Linking verbs
description_plain: Your child can use verbs correctly, including perfect tenses, changing moods, active/passive voice, subject-verb agreement, and linking verbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure pronouns have a clear antecedent and are appropriate in number and person.
description_plain: Your child can make sure pronouns clearly refer to the right noun and match in number and person.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use pronouns properly:
  • Intensive and reflexive pronouns
  • Relative pronouns
description_plain: Your child can use pronouns properly, including intensive, reflexive, and relative pronouns.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use nouns effectively:
  • Direct and indirect objects
description_plain: Your child can use nouns correctly as direct and indirect objects in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recite a passage from a well-known poem, play, or speech.
description_plain: Your child can memorize and recite parts of well-known poems, plays, or speeches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can use standard English correctly when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using appropriate eye contact, tone, and pacing.
description_plain: Your child can adjust how they speak for different situations by using good eye contact, tone, and speaking speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include relevant multimedia to clarify claims and findings.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or other media to help explain their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claims, central ideas, and/or themes with descriptions, facts, details, and examples.
description_plain: Your child will support their main ideas with facts, details, and examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sequence ideas logically.
description_plain: Sequence ideas logically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and evidence, emphasizing primary points in a focused, coherent manner.
description_plain: Your child can share their main points and evidence clearly and in an organized way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Articulate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the accuracy of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child understands a speaker’s argument and checks if the evidence is accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how central ideas and supporting details from non-textual sources clarify a topic, text, or issue.
description_plain: Your child explains how pictures, videos, or other sources help make a topic or issue clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and modify one's own views as needed.
description_plain: Your child listens to new information from others and changes their own ideas when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Follow rules for collegial discussions, tracking progress towards specific goals and deadlines, and defining individual roles as needed. Collegial discussions should include:
  • One-on-one
  • In small groups
  • Teacher-led
description_plain: Your child follows rules during discussions, keeps track of goals and deadlines, and understands their role whether talking one-on-one, in small groups, or with a teacher.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, referring to researched evidence on the topic, text, or issue.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared with facts and research about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Express ideas in a collaborative setting, using effective discussion strategies.
description_plain: Your child shares their ideas clearly and works well with others during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate an understanding of figurative language in context, including allusions and analogies.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figurative language like comparisons and references in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how word choice, including rhyme and repetition, contributes to the meaning, style, and/or tone of a text.
description_plain: Your child notices how word choices like rhyme and repetition affect the meaning and feeling of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine words with similar denotations, considering how their connotations and nuances impact the words' meanings.
description_plain: Your child looks at words with similar meanings and understands how small differences change their meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing from a range of effective techniques:
  • Use common Greek or Latin affixes and roots (i.e., morphology),
  • Trace the origins of words (i.e., etymology),
  • Use context,
  • Consult reference materials to clarify pronunciation and/or parts of speech, and/or
  • Use word relationships such as cause and effect, part to whole, and item into category to clarify the meaning of each word.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new or tricky words using word parts, origins, context, dictionaries, and word relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode and encode words, using knowledge of Greek combining forms and Latin prefixes, bases, and suffixes and connectives as needed.
description_plain: Your child reads and spells words by using knowledge of Greek and Latin word parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use general academic and content-specific words and phrases accurately.
description_plain: Your child uses common academic and subject-specific words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow a standard format for citation, including bibliographic information.
description_plain: Your child follows a standard way to list sources with all the needed information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote or paraphrase data and conclusions, crediting sources and/or authors.
description_plain: Your child quotes or restates information from sources and gives credit to the authors.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess the credibility and accuracy of sources.
description_plain: Your child checks if sources are trustworthy and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct research to explore a topic and/or answer a question, refocusing the inquiry for further research, investigation, or refinement.
description_plain: Your child researches a topic or question and adjusts their focus to learn more or explore new ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain formal style when appropriate, editing writing to include grade-appropriate conventions for publishing.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style when needed and edits their work to meet grade-level rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include headings, graphics, and various multimedia to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child adds headings, pictures, and other media to make their writing clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Increase independent writing with support and collaboration from peers and adults, employing the stages of the writing process (e.g., draft, revise, edit) with a focus on audience.
description_plain: Your child writes more on their own with help from others, using steps like drafting, revising, and editing while thinking about who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop writing stamina during single sessions and over extended periods of time.
description_plain: Your child builds the ability to write for longer times during one session and over several days.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence, to signal shifts from one time or setting to another, and/or to clarify the relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses different words and phrases to show order, changes in time or place, and connections between ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose precise words, phrases, and clauses to clarify relationships among ideas, claims, reasons, and/or evidence about a topic.
description_plain: Your child picks exact words and phrases to clearly show how ideas, reasons, and evidence relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of sentence types (i.e., simple, compound, complex, compound-complex).
description_plain: Your child writes sentences in different styles, including simple and complex ones.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize writing logically, constructing an introduction, body, conclusion, and/or reflection when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child organizes their writing with a clear beginning, middle, and end, adding reflections when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Construct clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to the task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in a way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write to express real or imagined experiences and/or events.
  • Establish the topic, context, narrative elements, and point of view and/or perspective.
  • Use relevant, descriptive details and precise language.
  • Develop well-structured event sequences.
  • Use narrative techniques (e.g., dialogue, pacing, description).
description_plain: Your child writes stories or real experiences by setting the scene, using details, organizing events, and including techniques like dialogue and description.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write to inform about a topic.
  • Introduce a topic with a preview of what is to follow.
  • Convey ideas, concepts, and information.
  • Choose relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, and examples.
description_plain: Your child writes to inform by introducing a topic, sharing facts and examples, and explaining ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Compose an argument, using clear reasons and supporting evidence.
  • Introduce claims.
  • Acknowledge alternate or opposing claims.
  • Support claims with credible sources.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments by stating their claims, recognizing other views, and supporting their ideas with trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the central idea in a nonfiction work from or about American literature (beginnings through 1850), explaining its historical and/or contemporary significance.
description_plain: Your child finds the main idea in nonfiction American literature from before 1850 and explains why it matters then and now.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast how two or more authors write about the same topic, including how key information is presented, how evidence is explained, and how facts are interpreted or promoted.
description_plain: Your child compares how different authors write about the same topic, including how they share facts and explain ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the argument and supporting claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is logical and if the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child evaluates if an author’s argument makes sense and if the evidence supports their claims well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast how an individual, issue, or idea is portrayed in a written text and in multimedia.
description_plain: Your child compares how a person, issue, or idea is shown in writing and in videos or other media.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine how an author distinguishes his or her purpose, point of view, and/or perspective in a text from alternate or opposing positions.
description_plain: Your child looks at how an author shows their purpose and point of view compared to other opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how the major sections of a text influence the overall structure and development of ideas, themes, or arguments.
description_plain: Your child explains how the parts of a text help build the main ideas, themes, or arguments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas impact the development of a text.
description_plain: Your child describes how people, events, and ideas connect and affect the development of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the theme in an original, adapted, or modernized drama, poem, folktale, or story from American literature (beginnings through 1850), explaining its historical and/or contemporary significance.
description_plain: Your child identifies the theme in American stories, poems, or plays from before 1850 and explains why it is important.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character with the historical account of the corresponding time, place, or character.
description_plain: Your child compares a fictional story about a time, place, or person with the real historical facts.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Recognize the differences between reading a text, listening to an audio recording of a text, and/or watching a multimedia version of a text; differences of which:
  • Literary elements are imagined when reading a text, and
  • Multimedia techniques are observed when listening and/or watching.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between reading a story, listening to it, and watching it, understanding that reading lets them imagine details while listening or watching shows them real sounds and visuals.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how an author develops multiple points of view and perspectives of characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters’ or narrators’ points of view in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how elements in the overall structure of a story, drama, or poem contribute to its meaning.
description_plain: Your child understands how parts of a story, play, or poem work together to help explain its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine how the interaction of literary elements impacts a story or drama, including how setting shapes the characters or plot or how imagery affects the mood of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how things like the setting or pictures in a story affect the characters, plot, or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate reading comprehension of age and grade-appropriate texts by speaking or writing.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand what they read by talking or writing about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite text evidence to support an analysis of what a text states, using background knowledge to justify inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find proof in a text to explain what it means and use what they already know to make good guesses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how a central idea and/or theme of a text is developed through supporting details.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the main idea or theme of a text by looking at the details that support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide an objective summary of a text.

description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly to separate adjectives, items in a series, phrases, and when talking directly to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose language that precisely expresses ideas, eliminating redundancy.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use prepositional phrases effectively:

  • Adverbial

description: Produce compound-complex sentences, using dependent clauses, subordinating conjunctions, conjunctive adverbs, correlative conjunctions, and coordinating conjunctions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correct misplaced and dangling modifiers.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use modifiers effectively:
  • Proper adjectives
  • Predicate adjectives

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbals (gerunds, participles, infinitives) correctly.
description_plain: Your child can use verb forms like gerunds, participles, and infinitives properly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use verbs effectively:
  • Shifts in mood and voice
  • Active and passive voice
  • Subject/verb agreement
  • Linking verbs
description_plain: Your child can use verbs correctly, including changing mood and voice, matching subjects and verbs, and using linking verbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure pronouns have a clear antecedent and are appropriate in number and person.
description_plain: Your child can use pronouns clearly so it’s easy to know who or what they refer to and match them properly.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use nouns effectively:
  • Direct and indirect objects
  • Predicate nouns
description_plain: Your child can use nouns correctly as objects and complements in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recite a passage from a well-known poem, play, or speech.
description_plain: Your child can recite a passage from a famous poem, play, or speech from memory.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can use correct standard English when speaking or writing as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using appropriate eye contact, tone, and pacing.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak by using the right eye contact, tone, and speed for different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include relevant multimedia to clarify claims and findings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claims, central ideas, and/or themes with valid reasoning, relevant evidence, and well-chosen details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and evidence, emphasizing important points in a focused, coherent manner.

subject: Language Arts
description: Articulate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the accuracy and adequacy of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain someone else’s argument and check if their evidence is correct and enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine the reasons for using multimedia to present a topic, text, and/or issue.
description_plain: Your child can explain why using multimedia is helpful when sharing information or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others, qualifying or justifying one's own views after new evidence is presented.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, tracking progress towards specific goals and deadlines, and defining individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules during group talks, keep track of goals and deadlines, and understand their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, citing researched evidence on the topic, text, or issue, and reflecting upon the discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Express ideas in a collaborative setting, using effective discussion strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate an understanding of figurative language in context, including verbal irony and puns.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can compare words with similar dictionary meanings and explain how their feelings or shades of meaning differ.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing from a range of effective techniques:
  • Use common Greek or Latin affixes and roots (i.e., morphology),
  • Trace the origins of words (i.e., etymology),
  • Use context,
  • Consult reference materials to clarify pronunciation and/or parts of speech, and/or
  • Use word relationships such as cause and effect, part to whole, and item into category to clarify the meaning of each word.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using word parts, origins, context, dictionaries, and word relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode and encode words, using knowledge of Greek combining forms and Latin prefixes, bases, and suffixes and connectives as needed.
description_plain: Your child can read and spell words by knowing Greek and Latin word parts and how they connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use general academic and content-specific words and phrases accurately.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow a standard format for citation, including a works cited/references.
description_plain: Your child can follow standard rules to list sources and create a works cited or references page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Avoid plagiarism when quoting or paraphrasing data and conclusions, crediting sources and/or authors.
description_plain: Your child understands how to avoid copying others' work by giving credit when using their ideas or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess the credibility and accuracy of sources.
description_plain: Your child can check if information sources are trustworthy and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct research to explore a topic, describe an idea, and/or answer a question, refocusing the inquiry to generate further questions or to allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can research a topic, explain ideas, and ask new questions to explore more.

description: Maintain formal style when appropriate, editing writing to include grade-appropriate conventions for publishing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include headings, graphics, and various multimedia to support ideas and information.
description_plain: Your child can add headings, pictures, and multimedia to support their writing.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can write more on their own, working with others to draft, revise, and edit for a clear purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop writing stamina during single sessions and over extended periods of time.
description_plain: Your child can write for longer times without stopping and build writing endurance.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses effectively to connect ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose precise words, phrases, and clauses to clarify relationships among ideas, claims, counterclaims, reasons, and/or evidence.
description_plain: Your child can pick exact words and phrases to clearly show how ideas and reasons relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of sentence types effectively.
description_plain: Your child can use different types of sentences to make writing interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize writing logically, composing an introduction, body, conclusion, and/or reflection when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can organize writing with a clear introduction, body, and conclusion or reflection when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Construct clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to the task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write to express real or imagined experiences and/or events.
  • Develop a topic, context, narrative elements, and point of view and/or perspective.
  • Select well-chosen, descriptive details and use sensory language and precise language.
  • Develop well-structured event sequences to show the relationships among ideas and experiences.
  • Use narrative techniques effectively, utilizing dialogue, pacing, sensory language, and description.
description_plain: Your child can write stories or personal experiences with clear topics, details, and events, using dialogue and descriptions to make the story come alive.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write to inform about a topic.
  • Introduce a topic with a clear preview of what is to follow.
  • Organize ideas, concepts, and information, using broad categories.
  • Select well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, and examples.
  • Maintain a cohesive structure, clarifying the relationships among the ideas, concepts, and information.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic by introducing it clearly, organizing facts and ideas, and using examples and details.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Compose an argument, using clear reasons and supporting evidence.
  • Introduce claims supported by credible sources.
  • Distinguish alternate or opposing claims.
  • Maintain a cohesive structure.
description_plain: Your child can write an argument by stating clear reasons, supporting them with evidence, and showing different points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the central idea in a non-fiction work from or about American literature (1850-1930), explaining its historical and/or contemporary significance.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in nonfiction American literature from 1850-1930 and explain why it matters then and now.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze two or more texts that present conflicting information on the same topic, identifying where the texts diverge on matters of fact or interpretation.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more texts that disagree on facts or opinions about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the argument and supporting claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is logical, if the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims, and when irrelevant evidence is introduced.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the efficacy of various multimedia used to present information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish how the structure of a text contributes to its overall meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine how specific lines of dialogue propel the plot, reveal aspects of a character, or inform a character's decision.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate reading comprehension of age and grade-appropriate texts by speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite text evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what a text states, using background knowledge to justify inferences drawn from the text.

subject: Language Arts

description: Provide an objective summary of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of reading foundational skills, spelling patterns, and generalizations such as syllable patterns, ending rules, and meaningful word parts (i.e., morphology) to spell correctly.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly by using rules about syllables, endings, and word parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link two or more closely related independent clauses, using a semicolon and conjunctive adverb when applicable.
description_plain: Your child can join related sentences using semicolons and connecting words when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a list, quotation, or clarification, using a colon.
description_plain: Your child can use a colon to introduce lists, quotes, or explanations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Set off restrictive, nonrestrictive, and parenthetical elements, using commas, parentheses, and dashes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use parallel structure.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use prepositional phrases effectively:
  • Adjectival

description: Produce compound-complex sentences, using dependent clauses, subordinating conjunctions, conjunctive adverbs, correlative conjunctions, and coordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child can write sentences that combine multiple ideas using different connecting words and clauses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correct misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child can fix sentences where descriptive words or phrases are placed incorrectly or confusingly.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use modifiers effectively:
  • Predicate adjectives
description_plain: Your child uses describing words that come after linking verbs to give more information about the subject.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbals (gerunds, participles, infinitives) correctly.
description_plain: Your child uses special verb forms like gerunds, participles, and infinitives correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Use verbs effectively:
  • Conditional and subjunctive mood
  • Shifts in mood and voice

description_plain: Your child uses verbs correctly to show different moods and voices, like active or passive, and changes in tone.

description: Ensure pronouns have a clear antecedent and are appropriate in number and person.

description: Demonstrate command of standard English when indicated or appropriate.

description_plain: Your child adds pictures, videos, or other media to help explain their ideas or evidence.

description_plain: Your child compares words with similar meanings and understands how feelings, changes over time, and regions affect their use.

Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing from a range of effective techniques:
  • Use common Greek or Latin affixes and roots (i.e., morphology),
  • Trace the origins of words (i.e., etymology),
  • Use context,
  • Consult reference materials to clarify pronunciation and/or parts of speech, and/or
  • Use word relationships such as cause and effect, part to whole, and item into category to clarify the meaning of each word.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using word parts, origins, context, dictionaries, and word relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode and encode words, using knowledge of Greek combining forms and Latin prefixes, bases, and suffixes and connectives as needed.
description_plain: Your child reads and spells words by knowing Greek and Latin roots, prefixes, and suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use general academic and content-specific words and phrases accurately, gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering unfamiliar words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow a standard citation format, including both in-text citations and a works cited or bibliography.
description_plain: Your child follows standard rules to show where information comes from, including in-text notes and a list of sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase and/or embed select quotations to maintain the flow of ideas, crediting sources and/or authors and avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child rewrites or includes quotes smoothly in their writing, giving credit to the original authors to avoid plagiarism.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess the credibility, accuracy, and usefulness of sources, using a scholarly database when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child checks if sources are trustworthy, accurate, and useful, using scholarly databases when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short and sustained research to answer a question or solve a problem, narrowing or broadening the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child does short or long research to answer questions or solve problems, adjusting the focus as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conform writing to style manual guidelines appropriate for the discipline and writing type, editing writing to include grade-appropriate conventions for publishing.
description_plain: Your child writes following style rules for the subject and type of writing and edits their work for correct grammar and format.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include headings, graphics, and various multimedia to support ideas and information.
description_plain: Your child adds headings, pictures, and other media to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in the writing process (e.g., draft, revise, edit), addressing what is most significant for a specific purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child works through writing steps like drafting and revising, focusing on what matters most for their audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write with stamina during single sessions and over extended periods of time.
description_plain: Your child writes for long periods without losing focus or energy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses effectively to connect ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to connect ideas smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose precise words, phrases, and clauses to clarify relationships among ideas, claims, counterclaims, reasons, and/or evidence.
description_plain: Your child picks exact words and phrases to clearly show how ideas, claims, and evidence relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of sentence types effectively.
description_plain: Your child uses different types of sentences effectively in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize writing logically, composing an introduction, body, conclusion, and/or purposeful reflection when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child organizes writing with a clear introduction, body, conclusion, and reflection when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Construct clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to the task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and logically, matching the style to the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write to express real or imagined experiences and/or events:
  • Develop the topic, context, narrative elements, and point of view and/or perspective.
  • Select well-chosen, descriptive details, sensory language, and precise language, conveying a detailed picture of conflict, setting, and/or characters.
  • Develop a well-structured event sequence and plot line to enhance the relationships among ideas and experiences.
  • Use narrative techniques effectively.
description_plain: Your child writes stories or descriptions about real or imagined events, using details, sensory language, clear point of view, and a well-planned plot.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write to inform about a complex topic:
  • Develop a topic with a clear preview of what is to follow.
  • Organize ideas, concepts, and information, using major sections
  • Select well-chosen facts, extended definitions, quotations, commentary, and/or examples, making connections and distinctions when appropriate.
  • Maintain a cohesive informational structure, clarifying the relationships among ideas, concepts, and information.
description_plain: Your child writes to explain a complex topic by organizing ideas clearly, using facts, examples, and definitions.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Compose an argument about a complex topic:
  • Develop claims supported by credible sources.
  • Distinguish alternate or opposing claims, including the strengths and weaknesses of each in a manner that anticipates the audience's knowledge and concerns.
  • Maintain a cohesive argumentative structure throughout.
  • Provide commentary to connect claims to the reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments about complex topics, supporting claims with good sources, considering opposing views, and connecting ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a central idea in a non-fiction work from or about Arkansas or regional literature, explaining its historical and/or contemporary significance.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in a non-fiction book or article about Arkansas or nearby areas and explain why it matters today or in history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more texts, noting how they address related themes and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more texts to see how they talk about similar ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the argument and supporting claims in a text, assessing the degree to which the evidence is relevant and sufficient, the reasoning is valid, and false statements are present.
description_plain: Your child can judge how well an author supports their argument by checking if the evidence makes sense, is enough, and if any false information is included.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare accounts of a subject told in different multimedia, determining which details are emphasized in each account.
description_plain: Your child can compare different types of media telling the same story and notice which details each one focuses on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how an author uses rhetoric to advance the purpose, point of view, or perspective of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author uses language to share their opinion or purpose in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Detail how an author uses structure to develop a central idea or claim through sentences, paragraphs, or larger portions of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes sentences and paragraphs to develop the main idea or claim.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author introduces and develops a central individual, event, or idea through description and sequencing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a theme in an original, adapted, or modernized drama, poem, folktale, or story from Arkansas or regional literature, explaining its historical and/or contemporary significance.
description_plain: Your child can find a theme in a story, poem, or play from Arkansas or nearby areas and explain why it is important now or in history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a contemporary or classical text reimagines and/or adapts the source material from which it was derived.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how a modern or classic story changes or reimagines the original story it came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the representation of a subject or key scene in two artistic mediums, including what is emphasized or absent in each treatment.
description_plain: Your child can compare how a subject or important scene is shown in two different art forms, noticing what each one highlights or leaves out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a character's point of view (POV) and/or perspective is shaped through experiences.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a character’s point of view or feelings change because of their experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how the overall structure and sequencing of a text enhances the mood, suspense, and tension.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can describe how a complex character grows, interacts with others, moves the story forward, or helps develop the theme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate reading comprehension of age and grade-appropriate texts by speaking or writing.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand reading materials appropriate for their age by speaking or writing about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite text evidence that most strongly and thoroughly supports an analysis of what a text states, using background knowledge to justify inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the best evidence from a text to support their ideas, using what they already know to understand the text better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how a central idea and/or theme is developed over the course of a text, identifying which supporting details most contribute to the development of a central theme and/or idea.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how the main idea or theme develops in a text and point out the details that help build it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide an objective summary of a text.

description:
Write to express real or imagined experiences and/or events:

  • Maintain a cohesive informational structure and objective tone, clarifying the relationships among ideas, concepts, and information.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts about complex topics with clear organization, facts, examples, and an objective tone.

  • Maintain a cohesive argumentative structure throughout.
  • Provide commentary to connect claims to the reasons and evidence.

description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly by using rules about syllables, endings, and meaningful word parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Separate elements in a sentence, using hyphens, commas, ellipses, dashes, and semicolons.
description_plain: Your child can use punctuation like hyphens, commas, ellipses, dashes, and semicolons to separate parts of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link two or more closely related independent clauses, using a semicolon and conjunctive adverb when applicable.
description_plain: Your child can join two or more complete sentences using semicolons and connecting words when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a list, quotation, or clarification, using a colon.
description_plain: Your child can use a colon to introduce a list, a quote, or an explanation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use parallel structure.
description_plain: Use parallel structure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use various types of phrases and clauses to convey specific meanings and add interest to writing and/or presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use different types of phrases and clauses to make their writing or speaking more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary syntax for effect, consulting references for guidance as needed.
description_plain: Your child can change sentence structure to create different effects and can use references for help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correct misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child can fix misplaced or unclear modifiers in sentences so the meaning is clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs effectively.
description_plain: Use verbs effectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure pronouns have a clear antecedent and are appropriate in number and person
description_plain: Your child can make sure pronouns clearly refer to the right nouns and match in number and person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recite a passage from a well-known poem, play, or speech.
description_plain: Your child can memorize and recite parts of well-known poems, plays, or speeches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can use standard English correctly when it is needed or expected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of audiences, contexts, and tasks, using appropriate body language, tone, style, and pacing.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak—including body language, tone, and speed—to fit different audiences and situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include relevant multimedia to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, videos, or other media to help explain their ideas and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claims, central ideas, and/or themes with valid reasoning, relevant evidence, and well-chosen details, addressing opposing perspectives as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can support their ideas with good reasons and evidence, and address other points of view when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims, findings, and supporting evidence concisely, logically, and appropriately according to purpose, audience, and task.
description_plain: Your child can clearly and logically share their ideas and evidence in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's perspective, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, assessing the effectiveness.
description_plain: Your child can judge how well a speaker presents their ideas and uses evidence and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate non-textual information from multiple and diverse multimedia, evaluating the credibility and accuracy of sources.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from different media sources and decide if those sources are trustworthy and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Synthesize comments, claims, and evidence made on multiple sides of an issue, resolving contradictions when possible and determining what additional information or research is needed.
description_plain: Your child can bring together different opinions and evidence on an issue, resolve conflicts if possible, and figure out what more information is needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Set and follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, tracking progress towards specific goals and deadlines, and defining individual roles as needed.
