description_plain: Your child can set and follow rules for group discussions and decisions, keep track of progress, and know their role.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared to participate and/or facilitate, citing researched evidence on the topic, text, or issue, and responding appropriately.
description_plain: Your child can come to discussions ready to share or lead by using researched facts and responding well to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Initiate and express ideas in a collaborative setting, using and adapting effective discussion strategies appropriately.
description_plain: Your child can start and share ideas in group talks, using good discussion skills and adjusting as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the role of figurative language in context, including hyperbole and paradox.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain how figurative language like exaggeration and paradox adds meaning in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine how word choice impacts the overall meaning, style, tone, or literary merit of a text, including how words affect a reader and/or audience.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the choice of words changes the meaning, style, tone, or quality of a text and how those words affect readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine words with similar denotations, considering how connotation and nuance impact meaning, can change over time, and may be influenced regionally.
description_plain: Your child understands how words with similar dictionary meanings can have different feelings or shades of meaning that change over time or in different places.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing from a range of effective techniques:
  • Use common Greek or Latin affixes and roots (i.e., morphology),
  • Trace the origins of words (i.e., etymology),
  • Use context,
  • Consult reference materials to clarify pronunciation and/or parts of speech or resolve issues of complex or contested usage, and/or
  • Use word relationships such as cause and effect, part to whole, and item into category to clarify the meaning of each word.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using word parts, word origins, context clues, dictionaries, and understanding how words relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode and encode words, using knowledge of Greek combining forms and Latin prefixes, bases, and suffixes and connectives as needed.
description_plain: Your child can read and write words by using knowledge of Greek and Latin word parts like prefixes, suffixes, and roots.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the major historical influences in the evolution of the English language (i.e., linguistics), including Old English, Middle English, and Modern English.
description_plain: Your child understands the main historical stages of the English language, including Old English, Middle English, and Modern English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use general academic and content-specific words and phrases accurately, gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering unfamiliar words or phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses academic and subject-specific words correctly and learns new vocabulary when they find unfamiliar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow a standard citation format, including in-text citations, literature review, and a works cited/references or bibliography.
description_plain: Your child can use a standard way to give credit to sources, including in-text citations and a list of references or bibliography.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase, integrate, and/or embed select quotations to maintain the flow of ideas, crediting sources and/or authors and avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can rewrite or include quotes smoothly in their writing, giving credit to the original authors to avoid plagiarism.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess the credibility and accuracy of sources, using scholarly databases when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can check if sources are trustworthy and accurate, using scholarly databases when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short and sustained research projects synthesizing information from multiple sources to answer a question or solve a problem, narrowing or broadening the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can do short or long research projects by gathering information from different sources to answer questions or solve problems, adjusting the focus as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conform writing to style manual guidelines appropriate for the discipline and writing type, editing writing to include grade-appropriate conventions for publishing.
description_plain: Your child can follow style rules for different types of writing and edit their work to meet grade-level standards before sharing it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include headings, graphics, and various multimedia to support ideas and information.
description_plain: Your child can add headings, pictures, and multimedia elements to support their ideas and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in the writing process (e.g., draft, revise, edit), addressing what is most significant for a specific purpose and audience.
description_plain: Your child can go through the writing process by drafting, revising, and editing, focusing on what matters most for their purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write with stamina over extended periods of time.
description_plain: Your child can write for long periods without losing focus or energy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses effectively to connect ideas.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to connect ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose precise words, phrases, and clauses to clarify relationships among ideas, commentary, claims, counterclaims, reasons, and/or evidence.
description_plain: Your child chooses exact words and phrases to clearly show how ideas, opinions, reasons, and evidence relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of sentence types effectively.
description_plain: Your child uses different types of sentences effectively in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize writing purposefully, composing an introduction, outline, major sections, and/or reflection appropriate to the task and purpose.
description_plain: Your child organizes their writing with a clear introduction, outline, main sections, and reflection that fit the task and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Construct clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to the task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and logically, using the right style and organization for the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write to express real or imagined experiences and/or events:
  • Develop the topic, context, narrative elements, and one or more points of view and/or perspectives.
  • Select well-chosen, descriptive details, sensory language, and precise language, conveying a detailed picture of conflict, setting, and/or characters.
  • Develop well-structured event sequences with multiple plot lines to enhance the relationships among ideas and experiences.
  • Use narrative techniques effectively.
description_plain: Your child writes stories or personal experiences by developing the topic, using descriptive details and sensory language, creating well-structured events, and using storytelling techniques.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Write to inform about a complex topic:
  • Develop a topic addressing the most significant and relevant evidence.
  • Organize ideas, concepts, and information, using major sections and subtopics.
  • Select well-chosen facts, extended definitions, quotations, commentary, and examples, making connections and distinctions when appropriate.
  • Maintain a cohesive informational structure and objective tone, clarifying the relationships among ideas, concepts, and information.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts on complex topics by focusing on important evidence, organizing ideas with sections and subtopics, using facts and examples, and keeping a clear and objective tone.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Compose an argument about a complex topic:
  • Develop defensible and significant claims.
  • Distinguish alternate or opposing claims, assessing the strengths and weaknesses of each in a manner that anticipates the audience's knowledge, values, and bias.
  • Maintain a cohesive argumentative structure throughout.
  • Provide relevant commentary to connect claims to the reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments on complex topics by making strong claims, considering opposing views, organizing ideas clearly, and explaining how reasons and evidence support their claims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify central ideas in works of literary nonfiction from world literary history, including works written after 1450.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas in important literary nonfiction works from world history written after 1450.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze documents of historical significance for their literary and rhetorical features, including documents from outside the U.S. when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child analyzes important historical documents, including those from other countries, by looking at their literary and persuasive features.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the premise, reasoning, and validity of an argument in texts, including works of public advocacy and seminal documents from outside the U.S.
description_plain: Your child evaluates the main idea, reasoning, and truthfulness of arguments in texts, including public speeches and important documents from other countries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate information presented in a variety of formats and from multiple sources, integrating the information that best answers a question or solves a problem.
description_plain: Your child evaluates information from different formats and sources, combining the best information to answer questions or solve problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how content, style, and/or use of rhetorical devices contribute to the purpose, point of view, perspective, and overall effectiveness of a text.
description_plain: Your child evaluates how the content, style, and use of persuasive language help achieve the purpose and point of view of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the syntax of a text, including the extent to which it impacts the clarity, credibility, and appeal of the information presented.
description_plain: Your child analyzes sentence structure to see how it affects the clarity, trustworthiness, and appeal of the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how an author develops and connects a complex set of central ideas and key details, including how the central ideas and key details evolve, interact, and contribute to the overall meaning of a text.
description_plain: Your child evaluates how an author develops and connects complex main ideas and details, showing how they change and work together to create meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify themes in works of British literature, noting the dominant traits from Medieval, Renaissance, Neoclassical, Romantic, Victorian, Modern, and Post-Modern periods.
description_plain: Your child identifies themes in British literature and recognizes key traits from different historical periods like Medieval, Renaissance, Romantic, and Modern.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify themes in works of British literature (1450-present), including the ways British writers incorporate ancient, mythological, and/or religious stories into their writings.
description_plain: Your child identifies themes in British literature since 1450 and notices how writers include ancient myths and religious stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more influential literary works from the same world era addresses similar themes.
description_plain: Your child compares how two or more important literary works from the same time period explore similar themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how various portrayals of a story, drama, or poem represent and/or interpret the original source material from which they were derived.
description_plain: Your child evaluates how different versions of a story, play, or poem represent or interpret the original work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine a text in which understanding the purpose, point of view (POV), and/or perspective requires the reader to distinguish between what the author states and what is implied through irony, sarcasm, understatement, and satire, including how this contributes to a character's point of view and/or perspective.
description_plain: Your child examines texts where understanding the author's purpose and point of view means noticing what is said directly and what is implied through irony, sarcasm, or satire.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how syntax contributes to the overall meaning and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how sentence structure adds to the meaning and style of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Examine how characterization, plot, setting, and other literary elements interact with and contribute to the development and complexity of a text.
description_plain: Your child examines how characters, plot, setting, and other story elements work together to develop a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate reading comprehension of age and grade-appropriate texts by speaking or writing.
description_plain: Your child shows understanding of age-appropriate texts by speaking or writing about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite text evidence that strongly and thoroughly supports an analysis of what a text states, what the details imply, and what ambiguities are left to interpret, using background knowledge to justify inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child finds strong evidence in a text to support their analysis of what it says, what it suggests, and what is left open to interpretation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how multiple complex central ideas and/or themes are developed over the course of a text, including how details interact and build upon one another.
description_plain: Your child figures out how multiple complex main ideas or themes develop and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide an objective summary of a text.
description_plain: Your child can write an objective summary that clearly explains the main points of a text.

subject: Math
description: Collect, sort, and organize data into two or three categories, using real-object graphs and picture graphs.
description_plain: Your child can collect and sort objects into two or three groups and show the results using simple graphs with pictures or real items.

subject: Math
description: Identify pennies and dimes by name and value.
description_plain: Your child can recognize pennies and dimes by their names and know how much each is worth.

subject: Math
description:
Understand concepts of time, recognizing that clocks and calendars are tools that measure time.
  • Concepts of time include: morning, afternoon, evening, today, yesterday, tomorrow, day, week, month, and year
description_plain: Your child understands time by using clocks and calendars and knows words like morning, afternoon, yesterday, and year.

subject: Math
description: Make direct comparisons of the length, capacity, weight, and temperature of objects, recognizing which object is shorter/longer, lighter/heavier, warmer/cooler, or holds more.
description_plain: Your child can compare objects to see which is longer, shorter, heavier, lighter, warmer, cooler, or holds more.

subject: Math
description: Compose and draw shapes found in the world using objects (e.g., straws, toothpicks, clay balls).
description_plain: Your child can make and draw shapes using things like straws, toothpicks, or clay balls.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and sort a variety of two and three-dimensional shapes using informal language to describe their similarities, differences, and other attributes.
description_plain: Your child can sort and talk about different flat and solid shapes by describing how they are alike or different.

subject: Math
description: Identify two-dimensional attributes of three-dimensional objects.
description_plain: Your child can find flat shape parts on solid objects.

subject: Math
description:
Name shapes correctly regardless of their orientation or overall size.
  • Shapes include: squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, hexagons, cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres
description_plain: Your child can name shapes like squares, circles, triangles, and cubes no matter how big they are or how they are turned.

subject: Math
description:
Describe the positions of objects and geometric shapes in the environment.
  • Terms include: inside, outside, between, above, below, near, far, under, over, up, down, behind, in front of, next to, to the left of, and to the right of
description_plain: Your child can describe where things are using words like inside, outside, above, below, near, far, and next to.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving addition and subtraction within 10, using objects, drawings, or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple addition and subtraction problems up to 10 using objects, drawings, or number sentences.

subject: Math
description: Use manipulatives and various strategies to fluently add and subtract within 10.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 10 using objects and different methods.

subject: Math
description: Use a drawing or equation to find the number that makes 10 when added to a given number.
description_plain: Your child can draw or write a number sentence to find the number that adds to another number to make 10.

subject: Math
description: Use objects or drawings to decompose numbers less than or equal to 10 into pairs in more than one way, recording each decomposition.
description_plain: Your child can break apart numbers up to 10 into pairs in different ways and write down each way.

subject: Math
description: Use objects, fingers, mental images, drawings, sounds, acting out situations, or verbal explanations to represent addition and subtraction from 0 to 10.
description_plain: Your child can show addition and subtraction from 0 to 10 using fingers, objects, drawings, sounds, acting, or talking.

subject: Math
description: Compare two whole numbers, using the terms greater than, less than, or equal.
description_plain: Your child can compare two whole numbers and say which is bigger, smaller, or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Use matching and counting strategies to compare the number of objects in one group to the number of objects in another group (0 to 10) using the terms greater than, less than, or equal.
description_plain: Your child can count and match objects in two groups up to 10 and say which group has more, less, or if they are the same.

subject: Math
description: Show equivalent forms of whole numbers up to 20 as groups of tens and ones, using manipulatives and drawings.
description_plain: Your child can show numbers up to 20 as groups of tens and ones using objects or drawings.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and represent whole numbers from 0 to 20.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and show numbers from 0 to 20.

subject: Math
description: Identify quickly a number of items in a set from 0 to 10 without counting.
description_plain: Your child can quickly tell how many items are in a small group up to 10 without counting one by one.

subject: Math
description: Identify the position of objects in a set using ordinal numbers (first, second, third, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can use words like first, second, and third to show the order of objects in a group.

subject: Math
description: Count a set of objects up to 20 using one-to-one correspondence, demonstrating that the last number stated indicates the number of objects in the set regardless of the arrangement.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 20 objects by touching each one once and knows the last number counted is the total.

subject: Math
description: Count to 100 by ones and tens; count forward by ones from any given number up to 100.
description_plain: Your child can count to 100 by ones and tens and can start counting from any number up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Ask and answer questions about the total number represented such as how many in each category and how many more or less in one category compared to another.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about how many items are in each group and which group has more or less.

subject: Math
description: Organize, represent, and interpret data with up to three categories (e.g., tally tables, picture graphs, bar graphs).
description_plain: Your child can organize and show data in up to three groups using charts like tally marks, picture graphs, or bar graphs.

subject: Math
description: Organize and analyze data.
description_plain: Organize and analyze data.

subject: Math
description: Count collections of like coins including pennies, nickels, and dimes to determine their total value up to 100 cents.
description_plain: Your child can count groups of pennies, nickels, and dimes to find out how much money they have up to 100 cents.

subject: Math
description: Identify coins by name and value, including penny, nickel, dime, and quarter.
description_plain: Your child can name pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters and know how much each coin is worth.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest hour and half hour using analog clocks; understand how to read hours and minutes using digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest hour and half hour on clocks with hands and understand digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Explore time and money values and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can explore and learn about time and money.

subject: Math
description: Order three objects by their length, indirectly comparing the lengths of two objects by using a third object.
description_plain: Your child can put three objects in order from shortest to longest by comparing them directly or using another object.

subject: Math
description: Express the length of an object as a whole number of units by laying multiple copies of a shorter object end-to-end, understanding that the length of one object is equal to the number of same-size units that span the object with no gaps or overlaps.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long something is by lining up smaller objects end to end without gaps or overlaps.

subject: Math
description: Investigate measurement with non-standard units.
description_plain: Your child can explore measuring things using non-standard units like blocks or paper clips.

subject: Math
description:
Create a composite shape using two-dimensional or three-dimensional shapes.
  • Two-dimensional include: rectangle, square, trapezoid, triangle, hexagon, half circle, and quarter circle
  • Three-dimensional include: cube, rectangular prism, cone, and cylinder
description_plain: Your child can make new shapes by putting together flat or solid shapes like squares, triangles, cubes, or cones.

subject: Math
description: Understand the difference between defining attributes (e.g., triangles are closed and three-sided shapes) and non-defining attributes (e.g., color, orientation, overall size), using that understanding to build and draw shapes that exhibit defining attributes.
description_plain: Your child understands what makes a shape a certain kind (like a triangle has three sides) and can build or draw shapes showing these features.

subject: Math
description: Analyze attributes of shapes to develop generalizations about their properties.
description_plain: Your child can look at shapes and notice their features to understand what makes them similar or different.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in an addition or subtraction equation relating three whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can find the missing number in addition or subtraction problems with three numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply understanding of the equal sign to determine if equations involving addition and subtraction are true or false.
description_plain: Your child can decide if addition or subtraction sentences are true or false by understanding the equal sign.

subject: Math
description: Develop and apply understanding of foundational algebraic concepts.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use basic ideas about algebra.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving addition of three whole numbers whose sum is less than or equal to 20.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life addition problems with three numbers that add up to 20 or less.

subject: Math
description:
Solve real-world problems involving addition and subtraction within 20.
  • Problem types include: adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing with unknowns present throughout the addition and subtraction problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life addition and subtraction problems within 20, like adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems.
description_plain: Solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate the relationship between addition and subtraction by solving problems, using an inverse operation.
description_plain: Your child can show how addition and subtraction are related by solving problems using the opposite operation.

subject: Math
description: Use concrete models or drawings to add within 100, including a two-digit number and a one-digit number as well as a two-digit number and a multiple of ten; relate strategy used to a written expression or equation and explain reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can use objects or drawings to add numbers up to 100, including adding a two-digit number with a one-digit number or a multiple of ten, and explain how they solved it.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations to add and subtract within 20.
description_plain: Your child can use simple rules to add and subtract numbers up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Use computational fluency to add and subtract within 20 using manipulatives and/or a variety of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 20 using objects or different methods.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fluently within 10 with mastery by the end of first grade.
description_plain: Your child will be able to add and subtract numbers up to 10 quickly and accurately by the end of first grade.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations using place value understanding and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can do math operations by understanding place value and using simple math rules.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares, describing the shares using the words halves, fourths, and quarters; understand that decomposing into more equal pieces creates smaller pieces.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into two or four equal parts and use words like halves and quarters to describe them, understanding that more parts mean smaller pieces.

subject: Math
description: Build a conceptual understanding of fractions.
description_plain: Your child understands the basic idea of fractions.

subject: Math
description: Compare two two-digit numbers using symbols (<, =, >) based on the value of tens and ones in the given numbers.
description_plain: Your child can compare two two-digit numbers using symbols like <, =, or > by looking at their tens and ones.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to compare numbers.
description_plain: Your child uses place value to compare numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use mental strategies to find 10 more or 10 less than a given two-digit number.
description_plain: Your child can quickly find 10 more or 10 less than a two-digit number in their head.

subject: Math
description: Use concrete models or drawings to subtract multiples of 10 from multiples of 10 (within the range of 10-90), relate the strategy to a written expression or equation, and explain the reasoning used to solve.
description_plain: Your child can use objects or drawings to subtract multiples of 10 from each other (between 10 and 90), write the math sentence, and explain how they solved it.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and represent whole numbers up to 120, using concrete models or drawings, word form, base ten numerals, and expanded form.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and show numbers up to 120 using objects, drawings, words, base ten numbers, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Explain the place value of ones and tens in two-digit numbers, using concrete models, diagrams, numbers, or words.
description_plain: Your child can explain what the tens and ones mean in two-digit numbers using objects, pictures, numbers, or words.

subject: Math
description: Understand the base ten place value system.
description_plain: Your child understands how the base ten number system works.

subject: Math
description: Skip count forward by multiples of fives within 120.
description_plain: Your child can count forward by fives up to 120.

subject: Math
description: Count forward and back within 120 by ones and tens from any given whole number.
description_plain: Your child can count forward and backward by ones and tens up to 120, starting from any number.

subject: Math
description: Extend the counting sequence.
description_plain: Extend the counting sequence.

subject: Math
description: Ask and answer simple put together, take apart, and compare problems, using information presented in the bar graphs, picture graphs, and line plots.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer simple questions about adding, taking apart, and comparing using information from bar graphs, picture graphs, and line plots.

subject: Math
description: Use bar graphs, picture graphs, and line plots to organize and represent data, interpreting data with up to four categories.
description_plain: Your child can make and understand bar graphs, picture graphs, and line plots with up to four groups of data.

subject: Math
description: Organize and analyze data.
description_plain: Organize and analyze data.

subject: Math
description: Count collections of mixed coins and solve real-world problems involving quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies within 99¢ and whole dollar amounts.
description_plain: Your child can count mixed coins like quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies up to 99 cents and solve real money problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals in half hours or hours.
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems involving adding and subtracting time in hours and half hours.

subject: Math
description:
Describe relationships of time.
  • Times include: seconds in a minute; minutes in an hour; hours in a day; days in a week; and days, weeks, and months in a year
description_plain: Your child understands how many seconds are in a minute, minutes in an hour, hours in a day, days in a week, and how days, weeks, and months make up a year.

subject: Math
description: Using an analog clock, tell and write time to the nearest five minutes using colon notation and indicate a.m. or p.m.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest five minutes on an analog clock and use a.m. or p.m.

subject: Math
description: Explore time and money values and concepts.
description_plain: Your child explores how time and money work.

subject: Math
description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares, counting the total number of squares to find the area.
description_plain: Your child can divide a rectangle into rows and columns of equal squares and count all the squares to find the area.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems to find the perimeter of polygons.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems to find the perimeter of shapes.

subject: Math
description: Explore the perimeter and area of shapes.
description_plain: Your child explores the perimeter and area of different shapes.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving lengths of the same units, using addition and subtraction within 100.
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems about lengths using addition and subtraction within 100.

subject: Math
description: Measure to determine how much longer or shorter one object is than another, expressing the length difference in terms of a standard length whole unit.
description_plain: Your child can measure how much longer or shorter one object is compared to another using whole units.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate how the length of an object does not change, regardless of the units used to measure it, by measuring the length of an object twice; use two different length units, describing how the two measurements relate to the size of the chosen unit.
description_plain: Your child can measure the same object twice using two different units and explain how the measurements relate to the size of the units.

subject: Math
description: Select appropriate measurement tools to estimate and measure the length of an object to the nearest whole inch or whole centimeters.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right tool to estimate and measure length to the nearest whole inch or centimeter.

subject: Math
description: Investigate measurement using rulers.
description_plain: Your child learns how to measure using rulers.

subject: Math
description:
Identify and describe three-dimensional shapes based on the shape, number of faces, number of edges, and number of vertices.
  • Shapes include: rectangular prisms, cubes, and square-based pyramids
description_plain: Your child can identify and describe 3D shapes like rectangular prisms, cubes, and square-based pyramids by their faces, edges, and corners.

subject: Math
description:
Identify, describe, and draw two-dimensional shapes.
  • Shapes include: triangles, regular pentagons, regular hexagons, and quadrilaterals (square, rectangle, trapezoid, parallelogram, rhombus)
description_plain: Your child can identify, describe, and draw 2D shapes like triangles, pentagons, hexagons, squares, rectangles, trapezoids, parallelograms, and rhombuses.

subject: Math
description: Analyze attributes of shapes to develop generalizations about their properties.
description_plain: Your child looks at shape features to understand their properties.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a group of objects up to 20 has an odd or even number of members; write an equation to express an even number as a sum of two equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a group of up to 20 objects has an odd or even number and write an equation showing an even number as two equal parts.

subject: Math
description: Develop and apply understanding of foundational algebraic concepts.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses basic algebra ideas.

subject: Math
description: Solve one and two-step real-world problems involving addition and subtraction within 100 in situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing unknowns in all positions.
description_plain: Your child can solve one- and two-step real problems with addition and subtraction within 100, including adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems.
description_plain: Solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Use concrete models, drawings, or equations to solve addition and subtraction problems within 1000.
description_plain: Your child can use objects, drawings, or equations to solve addition and subtraction problems within 1000.

subject: Math
description: Use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arrays with up to 5 rows and up to 5 columns; write an equation to express the total as a sum of equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can add to find the total number of objects arranged in rows and columns (up to 5 rows and 5 columns) and write an equation showing the total as equal groups added together.

subject: Math
description: Use a number line to solve addition and subtraction problems within 100.
description_plain: Your child can use a number line to solve addition and subtraction problems within 100.

subject: Math
description: Add up to four two-digit numbers with sums not exceeding 100 using strategies based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can add up to four two-digit numbers with totals up to 100 using place value and simple math rules.

subject: Math
description: Use computational fluency to add and subtract within 100 using strategies based on place value, properties of operations, or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers within 100 using place value, math rules, or by thinking about how addition and subtraction relate.

subject: Math
description: Use mental strategies to fluently add and subtract within 20 with mastery by the end of second grade.
description_plain: Your child will be able to add and subtract numbers up to 20 in their head easily by the end of second grade.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations using place value understanding and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can solve math problems using their understanding of place value and basic math rules.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.
description_plain: Your child understands that equal parts of the same whole can look different in shape.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares, describing the shares using the words halves, thirds, and fourths (or quarters).
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into 2, 3, or 4 equal parts and name them halves, thirds, or fourths.

subject: Math
description: Build a conceptual understanding of fractions.
description_plain: Your child understands the basic idea of fractions as parts of a whole.

subject: Math
description: Compare two three-digit numbers using symbols (<, =, >) based on the value of hundreds, tens, and ones in the given numbers.
description_plain: Your child can compare two three-digit numbers using <, =, or > by looking at hundreds, tens, and ones.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to compare numbers.
description_plain: Your child uses place value to compare numbers and decide which is bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: Mentally add 10 or 100 to a given number in the range of 100-900 and mentally subtract 10 or 100 from a given number in the range of 100-900.
description_plain: Your child can add or subtract 10 or 100 in their head to numbers between 100 and 900.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and represent whole numbers up to 1,000 using concrete models or drawings, number names, and a variety of expanded forms.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and show numbers up to 1,000 using objects, drawings, words, and expanded forms.

subject: Math
description: Identify the value of hundreds, tens, and ones place in a three-digit number.
description_plain: Your child can tell the value of the hundreds, tens, and ones places in a three-digit number.

subject: Math
description: Understand the base ten place value system.
description_plain: Your child understands how the base ten place value system works.

subject: Math
description: Count within 1,000 forwards and backwards by ones, tens, and hundreds from any given number.
description_plain: Your child can count forwards and backwards by ones, tens, and hundreds up to 1,000 starting from any number.

subject: Math
description: Extend the counting sequence.
description_plain: Extend the counting sequence.

subject: Math
description: Solve one and two-step problems, using categorical data represented with a scaled picture graph, scaled bar graph, and a line plot.
description_plain: Your child can solve one- and two-step problems using data shown in picture graphs, bar graphs, and line plots.

subject: Math
description: Represent a data set with multiple categories, using a scaled picture graph, scaled bar graph, and a line plot.
description_plain: Your child can show data with many categories using picture graphs, bar graphs, and line plots.

subject: Math
description: Organize and analyze data.
description_plain: Organize and analyze data.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals in minutes.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that add or subtract time in minutes.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest minute, using analog clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest minute using analog clocks.

subject: Math
description: Tell time and solve problems about elapsed time.
description_plain: Your child can tell time and solve problems about how much time has passed.

subject: Math
description: Solve one-step real-world problems involving liquid volumes and masses of objects in the same units, using all four operations.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple real-world problems about liquid amounts and object weights using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division.

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate liquid volumes and masses of objects using standard units.
description_plain: Your child can measure and estimate liquid amounts and object weights using standard units.

subject: Math
description: Multiply side lengths to find areas of rectangles with whole number side lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of rectangles by multiplying side lengths in real-world and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with whole number side lengths by modeling with unit squares and multiplying the side lengths to show the results are the same.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of rectangles by counting squares and multiplying side lengths to show they get the same answer.

subject: Math
description: Describe area as the number of unit squares that cover a plane figure without gaps and overlaps.
description_plain: Your child understands that area is the number of squares needed to cover a flat shape without gaps or overlaps.

subject: Math
description: Calculate the area of rectangles and liquid volume.
description_plain: Your child can calculate the area of rectangles and measure liquid volume.

subject: Math
description: Measure lengths of objects to the nearest half and quarter inch, using a ruler.
description_plain: Your child can measure lengths to the nearest half and quarter inch using a ruler.

subject: Math
description: Investigate measurement using rulers.
description_plain: Your child explores measuring objects using rulers.

subject: Math
description: Recognize rhombuses, rectangles, and squares as examples of quadrilaterals, identifying and/or drawing examples of quadrilaterals that do not belong to any of these subcategories.
description_plain: Your child can recognize rhombuses, rectangles, and squares as types of four-sided shapes and can find or draw other four-sided shapes that are different.

subject: Math
description: Identify perpendicular and parallel lines, as well as right angles in two-dimensional shapes and real-world surroundings or objects.
description_plain: Your child can identify perpendicular and parallel lines and right angles in shapes and real-life objects.

subject: Math
description: Understand that quadrilaterals in different categories may share attributes; those attributes (e.g., four equivalent sides) can define a larger category (e.g., quadrilaterals) or subcategory (e.g., rhombus and square).
description_plain: Your child understands that four-sided shapes can share features like equal sides, which help group them into categories like squares or rhombuses.

subject: Math
description: Analyze attributes of shapes to develop generalizations about their properties.
description_plain: Your child looks at shape features to understand and describe their properties.

subject: Math
description: Understand division as an unknown-factor problem.
description_plain: Your child understands division as finding a missing number in a multiplication problem.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in a multiplication or division equation relating three whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can find the missing whole number in multiplication or division equations with three numbers.

subject: Math
description: Develop and apply an understanding of foundational algebraic concepts.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses basic algebra ideas.

subject: Math
description: Solve two-step real-word situations using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, representing these problems using equations with a symbol standing for an unknown quantity.
description_plain: Your child can solve two-step real-world problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, writing equations with a symbol for the unknown number.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems using multiplication and division within 100 involving equal groups, arrays, partitive and measurement division.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-world multiplication and division problems with numbers up to 100 using equal groups, arrays, and different division methods.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems.
description_plain: Solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Identify arithmetic patterns including, but not limited to, patterns in an addition or multiplication table, explaining use of properties of operations appropriate to the pattern.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain patterns in addition or multiplication tables using math rules.

subject: Math
description: Use strategies to multiply one-digit numbers by multiples of 10 ranging from 10-90; strategies are based on place value and properties of operations (e.g., 9∙80,5∙60).
description_plain: Your child can multiply one-digit numbers by multiples of 10 (like 10, 20, up to 90) using place value and math rules.

subject: Math
description:
Apply properties of operations as strategies to multiply and divide.
  • Properties include: Distributive, Commutative, and Associative Properties of Multiplication
description_plain: Your child uses math rules like distributive, commutative, and associative properties to help multiply and divide.

subject: Math
description:
Use basic fact fluency to multiply and divide whole numbers with mastery by the end of third grade.
  • Knowing all products with factors up to and including 12 and the corresponding division facts from the products with factors up to and including 12.
  • Using strategies such as the relationship between multiplication and division (e.g., Knowing that 8∙5=40, one knows 40 ÷ 5 =8) or properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child will know multiplication and division facts up to 12 by 12 and use strategies like the link between multiplication and division to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use computational fluency to add and subtract three-digit whole numbers, using strategies and algorithms based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract three-digit numbers using different methods that rely on understanding place value and how addition and subtraction work together.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations using place value understanding and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child will use their knowledge of place value and math rules to do addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

subject: Math
description:
Use number lines and visual models to recognize and generate equivalent fractions, explaining how they are equivalent in real-world and mathematical situations.
  • Fractions include: denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8
description_plain: Your child can use number lines and pictures to find and explain fractions that are equal, using fractions with denominators like 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.

subject: Math
description: Develop and apply equivalent fraction understanding.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses the idea that some fractions are equal even if they look different.

subject: Math
description:
Decompose and compose a non-unit fraction a/b as the quantity formed by the sum of unit fractions.
  • Fractions include: denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8
description_plain: Your child can break down fractions like a/b into a sum of smaller fractions called unit fractions, using denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.

subject: Math
description:
Identify and represent a non-unit fraction as a number on the number line, including fractions greater than one.
  • Fractions include: denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8
description_plain: Your child can find and show fractions greater than one on a number line, using denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.

subject: Math
description:
Identify and represent a unit fraction as a number on the number line.
  • Fractions include: denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8
description_plain: Your child can find and show simple fractions (one part of a whole) on a number line, using denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.

subject: Math
description:
Partition squares, regular hexagons, and equilateral triangles into parts with equal shares, explaining the shares of each part as a unit fraction of the whole.
  • Fractions include: denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8
description_plain: Your child can divide shapes like squares, hexagons, and triangles into equal parts and explain each part as a fraction of the whole, using denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.

subject: Math
description:
Identify fractions as parts of a whole and parts of a collection or set.
  • Fractions include: denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8
description_plain: Your child can recognize fractions as parts of a whole object or parts of a group, using denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.

subject: Math
description: Build a conceptual understanding of fractions.
description_plain: Your child understands what fractions mean and how they work.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with the same numerator or denominator by reasoning about their size based on the same whole; use symbols (<, =, >) and justify the conclusion using visual fraction models, concrete objects, or words.
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions with the same top or bottom number by thinking about their size, using symbols like <, =, or > and explaining with pictures or objects.

subject: Math
description: Compare two four-digit numbers using symbols (<, =, >) based on the value of thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones in the given numbers.
description_plain: Your child can compare two four-digit numbers using <, =, or > by looking at thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to compare numbers.
description_plain: Your child uses place value to compare numbers and decide which is bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: Read and write whole numbers up to 10,000, using base ten numerals, word form, and a variety of expanded forms.
description_plain: Your child can read and write numbers up to 10,000 in digits, words, and expanded forms.

subject: Math
description: Identify the value of thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones place in a four-digit number.
description_plain: Your child knows the value of the thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones places in a four-digit number.

subject: Math
description: Round four-digit whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100, using place value understanding.
description_plain: Your child can round four-digit numbers to the nearest 10 or 100 by understanding place value.

subject: Math
description: Understand the base ten place value system.
description_plain: Your child understands how the base ten number system works.

subject: Math
description: Use a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit, solving problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions with like denominators using data presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can use a line plot to show measurements in fractions and solve addition and subtraction problems with fractions that have the same bottom number.

subject: Math
description: Collect and interpret data from observations, surveys, and experiments; represent data using frequency tables and scaled bar graphs.
description_plain: Your child can collect information from surveys or experiments and show it using tables and bar graphs.

subject: Math
description: Organize and analyze data.
description_plain: Organize and analyze data.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving distances, liquid volume, and masses of objects, including problems that require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems involving distances, liquid amounts, and weights, including changing bigger units into smaller ones.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving addition and subtraction of money, including the ability to make change.
description_plain: Your child can solve money problems that include adding, subtracting, and making change.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving time intervals that may cross the hour.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about time intervals, even when they cross over an hour.

subject: Math
description: Convert measurements of length, weight/mass, and liquid volume within the same system of measurement, metric and customary, expressing measurements from a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements of length, weight, and volume within the same system, like metric or customary, by converting bigger units into smaller ones.

subject: Math
description: Apply measurement knowledge to solve real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about measuring to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles and figures composed of two or more rectangles in real-world situations.
description_plain: Your child can find the area and perimeter of rectangles and shapes made of rectangles in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Calculate the perimeter of polygons, area of rectangles, and liquid volume.
description_plain: Your child can calculate the perimeter of shapes, the area of rectangles, and the volume of liquids.

subject: Math
description: Identify and/or draw lines of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure.
description_plain: Your child can find or draw lines of symmetry on flat shapes.

subject: Math
description:
Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel lines, perpendicular lines, or angles of a specified size, involving quadrilaterals and triangles.
  • Shapes include: quadrilaterals (trapezoid, parallelogram, rectangle, square, rhombus) and triangles (right, acute, obtuse)
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes like quadrilaterals and triangles by looking for parallel lines, right angles, and other angle types.

subject: Math
description: Identify and draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular and parallel lines, identifying these in quadrilaterals and triangles.
description_plain: Your child can identify and draw points, lines, line segments, rays, different types of angles, and parallel or perpendicular lines, and find these in shapes like quadrilaterals and triangles.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-word problems finding unknown angle measures, using addition and subtraction when an angle is decomposed into non-overlapping parts.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems to find missing angle sizes by adding or subtracting parts of angles.

subject: Math
description: Measure angles in whole-number degrees, using a protractor, drawing angles of specified measure.
description_plain: Your child can measure angles in whole degrees using a protractor and draw angles with a specific size.

subject: Math
description: Identify angles as geometric shapes that are formed where two rays share a common endpoint, understanding that angles are measured with reference to a circle so that an angle that turns through a 1/360 of a circle is called a "one-degree angle" and an angle that turns through n one-degree angles is said to have an angle measure of n degree.
description_plain: Your child understands that an angle is made where two rays meet and that angles are measured in degrees, which are parts of a circle divided into 360 equal parts.

subject: Math
description: Expand knowledge of shapes by analyzing sides and angles.
description_plain: Your child learns more about shapes by studying their sides and angles.

subject: Math
description: Generate a number or shape pattern that follows a given rule, identifying apparent features of the pattern that are not explicit in the rule itself.
description_plain: Your child can create number or shape patterns that follow a rule and notice extra features in the pattern.

subject: Math
description: Students develop and apply an understanding of foundational algebraic concepts.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses basic algebra ideas.

subject: Math
description:
Solve real-word problems involving the multiplication of a fraction by a whole number using visual fraction models or equations.
  • Fractions include: denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems by multiplying a fraction by a whole number using pictures or equations, with fractions like 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100 as denominators.

subject: Math
description:
Solve real-world problems involving the addition and subtraction of fractions; include mixed numbers with like denominators, using visual fraction models or equations.
  • Fractions include: denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems adding and subtracting fractions and mixed numbers with the same bottom number, using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Solve multi-step, real-world problems posed with whole numbers and having whole-number answers, using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division; include problems in which remainders must be interpreted and represent these problems using equations with symbols standing for the unknown quantity.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step real-world problems with whole numbers using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, including problems where remainders matter, and write equations with symbols for unknowns.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving multiplicative comparison, using drawings and/or equations with a symbol for the unknown number, and distinguish between multiplicative comparison and additive comparison.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-world problems comparing amounts by multiplication, using drawings or equations with unknown numbers, and can tell the difference between multiplying and adding comparisons.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems.
description_plain: Solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description:
Multiply a fraction by a whole number using visual fraction models and equations.
  • Fractions include: denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100
description_plain: Your child can multiply a fraction by a whole number using pictures and equations, with denominators like 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.

subject: Math
description:
Add and subtract fractions, including mixed numbers, with like denominators, using visual fraction models and equations.
  • Fractions include: denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions and mixed numbers with the same bottom number, using pictures and equations, with denominators like 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.

subject: Math
description: Use strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and the relationship between multiplication and division to divide whole numbers with four-digits by one-digit divisors; quotients should be with and without whole number remainders.
description_plain: Your child can divide four-digit numbers by one-digit numbers using place value and multiplication/division relationships, sometimes with remainders.

subject: Math
description: Use strategies based on place value and the properties of operations to multiply four-digit by one-digit whole numbers and two two-digit whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can multiply four-digit numbers by one-digit numbers and multiply two two-digit numbers using place value and operation rules.

subject: Math
description: Use computational fluency to add and subtract whole numbers up to 1,000,000 by using strategies and algorithms, including the standard algorithm, with mastery by the end of fourth grade.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract whole numbers up to 1,000,000 using different methods, including the standard way, and will master this by the end of fourth grade.

subject: Math
description: Find the factor pairs for a given number in the range of 1-100, identifying whether a number is prime or composite; determine whether a given whole number in the range of 1-100 is a multiple of a given one-digit number.
description_plain: Your child can find pairs of numbers that multiply to a given number between 1 and 100, tell if a number is prime or composite, and check if a number is a multiple of a one-digit number.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations, using place value understanding and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can do math operations using their understanding of place value and how operations work.

subject: Math
description: Apply decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can write fractions with denominators 10 or 100 as decimal numbers.

subject: Math
description: Add two fractions with denominators of 10 and 100 by expressing the denominator of 10 as an equivalent fraction with a denominator of 100.
description_plain: Your child can add two fractions with denominators 10 and 100 by changing the fraction with denominator 10 to an equivalent fraction with denominator 100.

subject: Math
description:
Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction (n·a)/(n·b), using visual fraction models, generating equivalent fractions using the principle a/b = (n·a)/(n·b).
  • Fractions include denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
description_plain: Your child understands that multiplying the top and bottom of a fraction by the same number makes an equal fraction, and can explain this using pictures with denominators like 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.

subject: Math
description: Develop and apply equivalent fraction understanding.
description_plain: Your child can find and use fractions that are equal to each other.

subject: Math
description:
Decompose fractions, including fractions greater than one and mixed numbers, into unit fractions, using concrete models, drawings, and/or the number line.
  • Fractions include denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.
description_plain: Your child can break down fractions, including those bigger than one and mixed numbers, into smaller parts called unit fractions using models, drawings, or number lines with denominators like 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 100.

subject: Math
description: Develop a conceptual understanding of fractions.
description_plain: Your child understands what fractions are and how they work.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to the hundredths place, using symbols (<, =, >) to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimal numbers to the hundredths place and use symbols like <, =, or > to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators using symbols (<, =, >) to record the results of comparisons (e.g., by creating common denominators or numerators or by comparing to a benchmark of 0, ½, 1).
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions with different top and bottom numbers using symbols like <, =, or > by finding common denominators or comparing to 0, ½, or 1.

subject: Math
description: Compare two five-digit whole numbers and six-digit whole numbers, using symbols (<, =, >) to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare five-digit and six-digit whole numbers and use symbols like <, =, or > to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to compare numbers.
description_plain: Your child uses place value to compare numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round five-digit and six-digit whole numbers to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round five-digit and six-digit numbers to any place value.

subject: Math
description: Read and write whole numbers up to 1,000,000 using base ten numerals, word form, and a variety of expanded forms.
description_plain: Your child can read and write whole numbers up to 1,000,000 in digits, words, and expanded forms.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that a digit in a given place represents ten times what it represents in the place to its right.
description_plain: Your child understands that a digit in one place is ten times bigger than the same digit in the place to its right.

subject: Math
description: Understand the base ten place value system.
description_plain: Your child understands how the base ten place value system works.

subject: Math
description: Use a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit solving problems involving all four operations with fractions (excluding division of a fraction by fraction) using data presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can use a line plot to show measurements in fractions and solve problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division (except dividing fractions by fractions) with the data.

subject: Math
description: Collect and interpret data from observations, surveys, and experiments; represent data using frequency tables, scaled bar graphs, and scaled line graphs.
description_plain: Your child can collect data from observations, surveys, or experiments and show it using frequency tables, bar graphs, and line graphs.

subject: Math
description: Organize and analyze data.
description_plain: Organize and analyze data.

subject: Math
description: Represent real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in the first quadrant on a coordinate plane, interpreting coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on a coordinate grid in the first quadrant to solve real-world and math problems and explain what the points mean.

subject: Math
description: Graph points with whole number coordinates on a coordinate plane in the first quadrant, explaining how the coordinates relate to the horizontal and vertical axes to describe the location of points in the plane.
description_plain: Your child can plot points with whole number coordinates on a coordinate grid and explain how the numbers tell the point's location.

subject: Math
description: Develop an understanding of the coordinate system.
description_plain: Your child understands how the coordinate system works.

subject: Math
description: Convert among different-sized standard measurement units within the same system, including both the metric and customary systems, and solve multi-step, real-world problems using conversions.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements between different units in the same system (like inches to feet or centimeters to meters) and solve multi-step real-world problems using these conversions.

subject: Math
description: Apply measurement knowledge to solve real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child uses measurement skills to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems by calculating volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms by adding the volumes of the non-overlapping parts.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of solid shapes made of two non-overlapping rectangular boxes by adding their volumes.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving the volume of rectangular prisms with whole number side lengths by applying the formulas (V = l·w·h or V = B·h) and the properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about the volume of rectangular boxes with whole number sides using formulas like length × width × height.

subject: Math
description: Measure volumes by counting unit cubes using cubic cm (cm³), cubic in (in³), cubic ft (ft³), and improvised units (u³).
description_plain: Your child can measure volume by counting unit cubes in cubic centimeters, inches, feet, or other units.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with fractional and/or mixed number side lengths by using models and multiplying the fractional side lengths showing that both strategies produce the same area.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of rectangles with fractional or mixed number side lengths by using models and multiplying the side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Solve the area of rectangles and volume of rectangular prisms.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems finding the area of rectangles and the volume of rectangular boxes.

subject: Math
description:
Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties with the focus on quadrilaterals and triangles when teaching hierarchies.
  • Shapes to include: quadrilaterals (trapezoid, parallelogram, rectangle, square, rhombus, kite) and triangles (right, acute, obtuse, scalene, isosceles, equilateral)
description_plain: Your child can group two-dimensional shapes like quadrilaterals and triangles based on their sides and angles, including shapes like trapezoids, rectangles, squares, and different types of triangles.

subject: Math
description: Expand knowledge of shapes by analyzing sides and angles.
description_plain: Your child learns more about shapes by looking at their sides and angles.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns given two rules, identifying the relationship between the corresponding terms by graphing the terms in the first quadrant of the coordinate grid.
description_plain: Your child can create two number patterns from two rules and show how the numbers relate by graphing them on a coordinate grid.

subject: Math
description: Write simple expressions that record calculations with numbers, interpreting numerical expressions without evaluating them.
description_plain: Your child can write simple math expressions to show calculations and understand what they mean without solving them.

subject: Math
description: Evaluate numerical expressions with parentheses or brackets and exponents with the base of ten, using the Order of Operations.
description_plain: Your child can solve math expressions with parentheses, brackets, and powers of ten by following the correct order of operations.

subject: Math
description: Develop and apply an understanding of foundational algebraic concepts.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses basic algebra ideas.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving the division of unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions, using visual fraction models and equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-world problems that involve dividing unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving the division of natural numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers using visual models and equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-world problems where dividing whole numbers results in fractions or mixed numbers, using visual models and equations.

subject: Math
description: Solve and create real-world problems involving multiplication of fractions and mixed numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve and make up real-life problems that involve multiplying fractions and mixed numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems.
description_plain: Solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply previous understanding of division to divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child can divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions using what they already know about division.

subject: Math
description: Use visual models and equations to multiply whole numbers by fractions and fractions by fractions, including mixed numbers and fractions greater than one.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and equations to multiply whole numbers by fractions and multiply fractions by fractions, including mixed numbers and fractions bigger than one.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and solve fractions as division problems, (a/b = a÷b), where a and b are natural numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands that fractions can be seen as division problems and can solve them that way.

subject: Math
description:
Add and subtract fractions with like and unlike denominators by using equivalent fractions [a/b = (n·a)/(n·b)] to create common denominators; include real-world problems.
  • Fractions include: mixed numbers
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with the same or different bottom numbers by finding common denominators, including with mixed numbers, and solve real-life problems using these skills.

subject: Math
description: Multiply and divide decimals to hundredths, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, or the relationship between multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide decimals up to two decimal places using models, drawings, and strategies based on place value and how multiplication and division work.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract decimals to the hundredths using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract decimals up to two decimal places using models, drawings, and strategies based on place value and how addition and subtraction work.

subject: Math
description: Calculate whole number quotients of whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors using strategies based on place value, properties of operations, divisibility rules, and the relationship between multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child can divide whole numbers with up to four digits by two-digit numbers using strategies based on place value, multiplication and division facts, and divisibility rules.

subject: Math
description: Use computational fluency to multiply multi-digit whole numbers by using strategies and algorithms, including the standard algorithm, with mastery by the end of fifth grade.
description_plain: Your child can multiply multi-digit whole numbers using different methods and the standard algorithm confidently by the end of fifth grade.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations using place value understanding and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can do math operations using their understanding of place value and properties of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use visual models to explain the product of multiplying a whole number by a fraction greater than and less than one.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures to explain what happens when you multiply a whole number by a fraction that is less than or greater than one.

subject: Math
description: Build a conceptual understanding of fractions.
description_plain: Your child understands the basic ideas behind fractions.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to thousandths based on the value of the digits in each place, using symbols (<, =, >) to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals up to three decimal places and use symbols like <, =, or > to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to compare numbers.
description_plain: Your child can compare numbers by understanding the value of each digit based on its place.

subject: Math
description: Apply place value understanding to round decimals to any place up to the thousandths.
description_plain: Your child can round decimals to any place up to three decimal places using place value understanding.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimals to thousandths, using base-ten numerals, word form, and a variety of expanded forms.
description_plain: Your child can read and write decimals up to three decimal places in numbers, words, and expanded forms.

subject: Math
description: Explain patterns in the number of zeros and/or the decimal point when multiplying or dividing a number by a power of 10, using whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10.
